1_1_1.NKJV: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

1_1_1.CSB17: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

1_1_1.NIV: The BeginningIn the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
1_1_1.NRSVCE: In the beginning when God created the heavens and the earth,

1_1_2.NKJV: The earth was without form, and void; and darkness was on the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the face of the waters.
1_1_2.KJV: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.
1_1_2.ESV: The earth was without form and void, and darkness was over the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the face of the waters.
1_1_2.NASB: The earth was formless and void, and darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the waters.
1_1_2.NLT: The earth was formless and empty, and darkness covered the deep waters. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.
1_1_2.WEB: The earth was formless and empty. Darkness was on the surface of the deep and God’s Spirit was hovering over the surface of the waters.
1_1_2.CSB17: Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness covered the surface of the watery depths, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.
1_1_2.AMP: The earth was formless and void or a waste and emptiness, and darkness was upon the face of the deep [primeval ocean that covered the unformed earth]. The Spirit of God was moving (hovering, brooding) over the face of the waters.
1_1_2.MSG: Earth was a soup of nothingness, a bottomless emptiness, an inky blackness. God's Spirit brooded like a bird above the watery abyss.
1_1_2.NIV: Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the waters.
1_1_2.NRSVCE: the earth was a formless void and darkness covered the face of the deep, while a wind from God swept over the face of the waters.

1_1_3.NKJV: Then God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.
1_1_3.KJV: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
1_1_3.ESV: And God said, “Let there be light,” and there was light.
1_1_3.NASB: Then God said, 'Let there be light'; and there was light.
1_1_3.NLT: Then God said, “Let there be light,” and there was light.

1_1_3.NIV: And God said, “Let there be light,” and there was light.
1_1_3.NRSVCE: Then God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.

1_1_4.NKJV: And God saw the light, that it was good; and God divided the light from the darkness.
1_1_4.KJV: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness. the light from: Heb. between the light and between the darkness
1_1_4.ESV: And God saw that the light was good. And God separated the light from the darkness.
1_1_4.NASB: God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
1_1_4.NLT: And God saw that the light was good. Then he separated the light from the darkness.

1_1_4.CSB17: God saw that the light was good, and God separated the light from the darkness.

1_1_4.NIV: God saw that the light was good, and he separated the light from the darkness.
1_1_4.NRSVCE: And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.

1_1_5.NKJV: God called the light Day, and the darkness He called Night. So the evening and the morning were the first day.
1_1_5.KJV: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day. And the evening: Heb. And the evening was, and the morning was
1_1_5.ESV: God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, the first day.

1_1_5.CSB17: God called the light “day,” and the darkness he called “night.” There was an evening, and there was a morning: one day.

1_1_5.MSG: God named the light Day,he named the dark Night.It was evening, it was morning—Day One.
1_1_5.NIV: God called the light “day,” and the darkness he called “night.” And there was evening, and there was morning—the first day.
1_1_5.NRSVCE: God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, the first day.

1_1_6.NKJV: Then God said, “Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.”
1_1_6.KJV: And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters. firmament: Heb. expansion
1_1_6.ESV: And God said, “Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters.”
1_1_6.NASB: Then God said, 'Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters.'
1_1_6.NLT: Then God said, “Let there be a space between the waters, to separate the waters of the heavens from the waters of the earth.”

1_1_6.CSB17: Then God said, “Let there be an expanse between the waters, separating water from water.”
1_1_6.AMP: And God said, "Let there be an expanse [of the sky] in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters [below the expanse] from the waters [above the expanse]."
1_1_6.MSG: God spoke: "Sky! In the middle of the waters;separate water from water!"
1_1_6.NIV: And God said, “Let there be an expanse between the waters to separate water from water.”
1_1_6.NRSVCE: And God said, “Let there be a dome in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters.”

1_1_7.NKJV: Thus God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament; and it was so.

1_1_7.NLT: And that is what happened. God made this space to separate the waters of the earth from the waters of the heavens.

1_1_7.CSB17: So God made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above the expanse. And it was so.
1_1_7.AMP: And God made the expanse [of sky] and separated the waters which were under the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse; and it was so [just as He commanded].
1_1_7.MSG: God made sky.He separated the water under skyfrom the water above sky.And there it was:
1_1_7.NIV: So God made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above it. And it was so.
1_1_7.NRSVCE: So God made the dome and separated the waters that were under the dome from the waters that were above the dome. And it was so.

1_1_8.NKJV: And God called the firmament Heaven. So the evening and the morning were the second day.

1_1_8.NASB: God called the expanse heaven. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.

1_1_9.NKJV: Then God said, “Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear”; and it was so.

1_1_9.NLT: Then God said, “Let the waters beneath the sky flow together into one place, so dry ground may appear.” And that is what happened.

1_2_5.NIV: and no shrub of the field had yet appeared on the earth and no plant of the field had yet sprung up, for the Lord God had not sent rain on the earth and there was no man to work the ground,
1_2_5.NRSVCE: when no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up — for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was no one to till the ground;

1_2_6.NKJV: but a mist went up from the earth and watered the whole face of the ground.
1_2_6.KJV: But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground. there: or, a mist which went up from, etc.
1_2_6.ESV: and a mist was going up from the land and was watering the whole face of the ground —
1_2_6.NASB: But a mist used to rise from the earth and water the whole surface of the ground.
1_2_6.NLT: Instead, springs came up from the ground and watered all the land.
1_2_6.WEB: but a mist went up from the earth, and watered the whole surface of the ground.
1_2_6.CSB17: But mist would come up from the earth and water all the ground.
1_2_6.AMP: but a mist (fog, dew, vapor) used to rise from the land and water the entire surface of the ground—
1_2_6.MSG: (the whole Earth was watered by underground springs)—
1_2_6.NIV: but streams came up from the earth and watered the whole surface of the ground—
1_2_6.NRSVCE: but a stream would rise from the earth, and water the whole face of the ground —

1_2_7.NKJV: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living being.
1_2_7.KJV: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul. of the dust: Heb. dust of the ground
1_2_7.ESV: then the LORD God formed the man of dust from the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living creature.
1_2_7.NASB: Then the LORD God formed man of dust from the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living being.
1_2_7.NLT: Then the LORD God formed the man from the dust of the ground. He breathed the breath of life into the man’s nostrils, and the man became a living person.
1_2_7.WEB: Yahweh God formed man from the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
1_2_7.CSB17: Then the LORD God formed the man out of the dust from the ground and breathed the breath of life into his nostrils, and the man became a living being.
1_2_7.AMP: then the LORD God formed [that is, created the body of] man from the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and the man became a living being [an individual complete in body and spirit].
1_2_7.MSG: God formed Man out of dirt from the ground and blew into his nostrils the breath of life. The Man came alive—a living soul!
1_2_7.NIV: the Lord God formed the man from the dust of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living being.
1_2_7.NRSVCE: then the LORD God formed man from the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and the man became a living being.

1_2_8.NKJV: The LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden, and there He put the man whom He had formed.
1_2_8.KJV: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
1_2_8.ESV: And the LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and there he put the man whom he had formed.
1_2_8.NASB: The LORD God planted a garden toward the east, in Eden; and there He placed the man whom He had formed.
1_2_8.NLT: Then the LORD God planted a garden in Eden in the east, and there he placed the man he had made.
1_2_8.WEB: Yahweh God planted a garden eastward, in Eden, and there he put the man whom he had formed.
1_2_8.CSB17: The LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and there he placed the man he had formed.
1_2_8.AMP: And the LORD God planted a garden (oasis) in the east, in Eden (delight, land of happiness); and He put the man whom He had formed (created) there.
1_2_8.MSG: Then God planted a garden in Eden, in the east. He put the Man he had just made in it.
1_2_8.NIV: Now the Lord God had planted a garden in the east, in Eden; and there he put the man he had formed.
1_2_8.NRSVCE: And the LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east; and there he put the man whom he had formed.

1_2_9.NKJV: And out of the ground the LORD God made every tree grow that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.KJV: And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.ESV: And out of the ground the LORD God made to spring up every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.NASB: Out of the ground the LORD God caused to grow every tree that is pleasing to the sight and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.NLT: The LORD God made all sorts of trees grow up from the ground — trees that were beautiful and that produced delicious fruit. In the middle of the garden he placed the tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.WEB: Out of the ground Yahweh God made every tree to grow that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food, including the tree of life in the middle of the garden and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.CSB17: The LORD God caused to grow out of the ground every tree pleasing in appearance and good for food, including the tree of life in the middle of the garden, as well as the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.AMP: And [in that garden] the LORD God caused to grow from the ground every tree that is desirable and pleasing to the sight and good (suitable, pleasant) for food; the tree of life was also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the [experiential] knowledge (recognition) of [the difference between] good and evil.
1_2_9.MSG: God made all kinds of trees grow from the ground, trees beautiful to look at and good to eat. The Tree-of-Life was in the middle of the garden, also the Tree-of-Knowledge-of-Good-and-Evil.
1_2_9.NIV: And the Lord God made all kinds of trees grow out of the ground—trees that were pleasing to the eye and good for food. In the middle of the garden were the tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_9.NRSVCE: Out of the ground the LORD God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food, the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

1_2_10.NKJV: Now a river went out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it parted and became four riverheads.
1_2_10.KJV: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.
1_2_10.ESV: A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden, and there it divided and became four rivers.
1_2_10.NASB: Now a river flowed out of Eden to water the garden; and from there it divided and became four rivers.
1_2_10.NLT: A river flowed from the land of Eden, watering the garden and then dividing into four branches.
1_2_10.WEB: A river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from there it was parted, and became the source of four rivers.
1_2_10.CSB17: A river went out from Eden to water the garden. From there it divided and became the source of four rivers.
1_2_10.AMP: Now a river flowed out of Eden to water the garden; and from there it divided and became four [branching] rivers.
1_2_10.MSG: A river flows out of Eden to water the garden and from there divides into four rivers.
1_2_10.NIV: A river watering the garden flowed from Eden; from there it was separated into four headwaters.
1_2_10.NRSVCE: A river flows out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides and becomes four branches.

1_2_11.NKJV: The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one which skirts the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_11.KJV: The name of the first is Pison: that is it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
1_2_11.ESV: The name of the first is the Pishon. It is the one that flowed around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_11.NASB: The name of the first is Pishon; it flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_11.NLT: The first branch, called the Pishon, flowed around the entire land of Havilah, where gold is found.
1_2_11.WEB: The name of the first is Pishon: it flows through the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
1_2_11.CSB17: The name of the first is Pishon, which flows through the entire land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_11.AMP: The first [river] is named Pishon; it flows around the entire land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_11.MSG: The first is named Pishon; it flows through Havilah where there is gold.
1_2_11.NIV: The name of the first is the Pishon; it winds through the entire land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_11.NRSVCE: The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;

1_2_12.NKJV: And the gold of that land is good. Bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
1_2_12.KJV: And the gold of that land is good: there is bdellium and the onyx stone.
1_2_12.ESV: And the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
1_2_12.NASB: The gold of that land is good; the bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
1_2_12.NLT: The gold of that land is exceptionally pure; aromatic resin and onyx stone are also found there.
1_2_12.WEB: and the gold of that land is good. Bdellium and onyx stone are also there.
1_2_12.CSB17: Gold from that land is pure; bdellium and onyx are also there.
1_2_12.AMP: The gold of that land is good; bdellium (a fragrant, valuable resin) and the onyx stone are found there.
1_2_12.MSG: The gold of this land is good. The land is also known for a sweet-scented resin and the onyx stone.
1_2_12.NIV: (The gold of that land is good; aromatic resin and onyx are also there.)
1_2_12.NRSVCE: and the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.

1_2_13.NKJV: The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one which goes around the whole land of Cush.
1_2_13.KJV: And the name of the second river is Gihon: the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia. Ethiopia: Heb. Cush
1_2_13.ESV: The name of the second river is the Gihon. It is the one that flowed around the whole land of Cush.
1_2_13.NASB: The name of the second river is Gihon; it flows around the whole land of Cush.
1_2_13.NLT: The second branch, called the Gihon, flowed around the entire land of Cush.
1_2_13.WEB: The name of the second river is Gihon. It is the same river that flows through the whole land of Cush.
1_2_13.CSB17: The name of the second river is Gihon, which flows through the entire land of Cush.
1_2_13.AMP: The name of the second river is Gihon; it flows around the entire land of Cush [in Mesopotamia].
1_2_13.MSG: The second river is named Gihon; it flows through the land of Cush.
1_2_13.NIV: The name of the second river is the Gihon; it winds through the entire land of Cush.
1_2_13.NRSVCE: The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Cush.

1_2_14.NKJV: The name of the third river is Hiddekel; it is the one which goes toward the east of Assyria. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.KJV: And the name of the third river is Hiddekel: that is it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river is Euphrates. toward: or, eastward to Assyria
1_2_14.ESV: And the name of the third river is the Tigris, which flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.NASB: The name of the third river is Tigris; it flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.NLT: The third branch, called the Tigris, flowed east of the land of Asshur. The fourth branch is called the Euphrates.
1_2_14.WEB: The name of the third river is Hiddekel. This is the one which flows in front of Assyria. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.CSB17: The name of the third river is Tigris, which runs east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.AMP: The third river is named Hiddekel (Tigris); it flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.MSG: The third river is named Hiddekel and flows east of Assyria. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.NIV: The name of the third river is the Tigris; it runs along the east side of Asshur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_14.NRSVCE: The name of the third river is Tigris, which flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.

1_2_15.NKJV: Then the LORD God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to tend and keep it.
1_2_15.KJV: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it. the man: or, Adam
1_2_15.ESV: The LORD God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to work it and keep it.
1_2_15.NASB: Then the LORD God took the man and put him into the garden of Eden to cultivate it and keep it.
1_2_15.NLT: The LORD God placed the man in the Garden of Eden to tend and watch over it.
1_2_15.WEB: Yahweh God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to cultivate and keep it.
1_2_15.CSB17: The LORD God took the man and placed him in the garden of Eden to work it and watch over it.
1_2_15.AMP: So the LORD God took the man [He had made] and settled him in the Garden of Eden to cultivate and keep it.
1_2_15.MSG: God took the Man and set him down in the Garden of Eden to work the ground and keep it in order.
1_2_15.NIV: The Lord God took the man and put him in the Garden of Eden to work it and take care of it.
1_2_15.NRSVCE: The LORD God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and keep it.

1_2_16.NKJV: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, “Of every tree of the garden you may freely eat;
1_2_16.KJV: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat: thou: Heb. eating thou shalt eat
1_2_16.ESV: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, “You may surely eat of every tree of the garden,
1_2_16.NASB: The LORD God commanded the man, saying, 'From any tree of the garden you may eat freely;
1_2_16.NLT: But the LORD God warned him, “You may freely eat the fruit of every tree in the garden —
1_2_16.WEB: Yahweh God commanded the man, saying, “You may freely eat of every tree of the garden;
1_2_16.CSB17: And the LORD God commanded the man, “You are free to eat from any tree of the garden,
1_2_16.AMP: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, "You may freely (unconditionally) eat [the fruit] from every tree of the garden;
1_2_16.MSG: God commanded the Man, "You can eat from any tree in the garden,
1_2_16.NIV: And the Lord God commanded the man, “You are free to eat from any tree in the garden;
1_2_16.NRSVCE: And the LORD God commanded the man, “You may freely eat of every tree of the garden;

1_2_17.NKJV: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.”
1_2_17.KJV: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die. thou shalt surely: Heb. dying thou shalt die
1_2_17.ESV: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.”
1_2_17.NASB: but from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat from it you will surely die.'
1_2_17.NLT: except the tree of the knowledge of good and evil. If you eat its fruit, you are sure to die.”
1_2_17.WEB: but you shall not eat of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil; for in the day that you eat of it, you will surely die.”
1_2_17.CSB17: but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, for on the day you eat from it, you will certainly die.”
1_2_17.AMP: but [only] from the tree of the knowledge (recognition) of good and evil you shall not eat, otherwise on the day that you eat from it, you shall most certainly die [because of your disobedience]."
1_2_17.MSG: except from the Tree-of-Knowledge-of-Good-and-Evil. Don't eat from it. The moment you eat from that tree, you're dead."
1_2_17.NIV: but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, for when you eat of it you will surely die.”
1_2_17.NRSVCE: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall die.”

1_2_18.NKJV: And the LORD God said, “It is not good that man should be alone; I will make him a helper comparable to him.”
1_2_18.KJV: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him. meet: Heb. as before him
1_2_18.ESV: Then the LORD God said, “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him.”
1_2_18.NASB: Then the LORD God said, 'It is not good for the man to be alone; I will make him a helper suitable for him.'
1_2_18.NLT: Then the LORD God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper who is just right for him.”
1_2_18.WEB: Yahweh God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make him a helper comparable to him.”
1_2_18.CSB17: Then the LORD God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper corresponding to him.”
1_2_18.AMP: Now the LORD God said, "It is not good (beneficial) for the man to be alone; I will make him a helper [one who balances him—a counterpart who is] suitable and complementary for him."
1_2_18.MSG: God said, "It's not good for the Man to be alone; I'll make him a helper, a companion."
1_2_18.NIV: The Lord God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him.”
1_2_18.NRSVCE: Then the LORD God said, “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper as his partner.”

1_2_19.NKJV: Out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to Adam to see what he would call them. And whatever Adam called each living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.KJV: And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof. Adam: or, the man
1_2_19.ESV: Now out of the ground the LORD God had formed every beast of the field and every bird of the heavens and brought them to the man to see what he would call them. And whatever the man called every living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.NASB: Out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the sky, and brought [them] to the man to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called a living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.NLT: So the LORD God formed from the ground all the wild animals and all the birds of the sky. He brought them to the man to see what he would call them, and the man chose a name for each one.
1_2_19.WEB: Out of the ground Yahweh God formed every animal of the field, and every bird of the sky, and brought them to the man to see what he would call them. Whatever the man called every living creature became its name.
1_2_19.CSB17: The LORD God formed out of the ground every wild animal and every bird of the sky, and brought each to the man to see what he would call it. And whatever the man called a living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.AMP: So the LORD God formed out of the ground every animal of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to Adam to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called a living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.MSG: So God formed from the dirt of the ground all the animals of the field and all the birds of the air. He brought them to the Man to see what he would name them. Whatever the Man called each living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.NIV: Now the Lord God had formed out of the ground all the beasts of the field and all the birds of the air. He brought them to the man to see what he would name them; and whatever the man called each living creature, that was its name.
1_2_19.NRSVCE: So out of the ground the LORD God formed every animal of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to the man to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called every living creature, that was its name.

1_2_21.NKJV: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall on Adam, and he slept; and He took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh in its place.
1_2_21.KJV: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;
1_2_21.ESV: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and while he slept took one of his ribs and closed up its place with flesh.
1_2_21.NASB: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; then He took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh at that place.
1_2_21.NLT: So the LORD God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep. While the man slept, the LORD God took out one of the man’s ribs and closed up the opening.
1_2_21.WEB: Yahweh God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep. As the man slept, he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh in its place.
1_2_21.CSB17: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to come over the man, and he slept. God took one of his ribs and closed the flesh at that place.
1_2_21.AMP: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam; and while he slept, He took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh at that place.
1_2_21.MSG: God put the Man into a deep sleep. As he slept he removed one of his ribs and replaced it with flesh.
1_2_21.NIV: So the Lord God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep; and while he was sleeping, he took one of the man’s ribs and closed up the place with flesh.
1_2_21.NRSVCE: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; then he took one of his ribs and closed up its place with flesh.

1_2_22.NKJV: Then the rib which the LORD God had taken from man He made into a woman, and He brought her to the man.
1_2_22.KJV: And the rib, which the LORD God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man. made: Heb. builded
1_2_22.ESV: And the rib that the LORD God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
1_2_22.NASB: The LORD God fashioned into a woman the rib which He had taken from the man, and brought her to the man.
1_2_22.NLT: Then the LORD God made a woman from the rib, and he brought her to the man.
1_2_22.WEB: Yahweh God made a woman from the rib which he had taken from the man, and brought her to the man.
1_2_22.CSB17: Then the LORD God made the rib he had taken from the man into a woman and brought her to the man.
1_2_22.AMP: And the rib which the LORD God had taken from the man He made (fashioned, formed) into a woman, and He brought her and presented her to the man.
1_2_22.MSG: God then used the rib that he had taken from the Man to make Woman and presented her to the Man.
1_2_22.NIV: Then the Lord God made a woman from the rib he had taken out of the man, and he brought her to the man.
1_2_22.NRSVCE: And the rib that the LORD God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.

1_2_23.NKJV: And Adam said: “This is now bone of my bones And flesh of my flesh; She shall be called Woman, Because she was taken out of Man.”
1_2_23.KJV: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man. Woman: Heb. Isha Man: Heb. Ish
1_2_23.ESV: Then the man said, “This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.”
1_2_23.NASB: The man said, 'This is now bone of my bones, And flesh of my flesh; She shall be called Woman, Because she was taken out of Man.'
1_2_23.NLT: “At last!” the man exclaimed. “This one is bone from my bone,and flesh from my flesh!She will be called ‘woman,’because she was taken from ‘man.’”
1_2_23.WEB: The man said, “This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh. She will be called ‘woman,’ because she was taken out of Man.”
1_2_23.CSB17: And the man said: This one, at last, is bone of my bone and flesh of my flesh; this one will be called “woman,” for she was taken from man.
1_2_23.AMP: Then Adam said, "This is now bone of my bones, And flesh of my flesh; She shall be called Woman, Because she was taken out of Man."
1_2_23.MSG: The Man said,"Finally! Bone of my bone,flesh of my flesh!Name her Womanfor she was made from Man."
1_2_23.NIV: The man said,“This is now bone of my bonesand flesh of my flesh;she shall be called ‘woman,’for she was taken out of man.”
1_2_23.NRSVCE: Then the man said, “This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; this one shall be called Woman, for out of Man this one was taken.”

1_2_24.NKJV: Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and they shall become one flesh.
1_2_24.KJV: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
1_2_24.ESV: Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and hold fast to his wife, and they shall become one flesh.
1_2_24.NASB: For this reason a man shall leave his father and his mother, and be joined to his wife; and they shall become one flesh.
1_2_24.NLT: This explains why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife, and the two are united into one.
1_2_24.WEB: Therefore a man will leave his father and his mother, and will join with his wife, and they will be one flesh.
1_2_24.CSB17: This is why a man leaves his father and mother and bonds with his wife, and they become one flesh.

1_4_9.NLT: Afterward the LORD asked Cain, “Where is your brother? Where is Abel?” “I don’t know,” Cain responded. “Am I my brother’s guardian?”
1_4_9.WEB: Yahweh said to Cain, “Where is Abel, your brother?” He said, “I don’t know. Am I my brother’s keeper?”
1_4_9.CSB17: Then the LORD said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?” “I don’t know,” he replied. “Am I my brother’s guardian?”
1_4_9.AMP: Then the LORD said to Cain, "Where is Abel your brother?" And he [lied and] said, "I do not know. Am I my brother's keeper?"
1_4_9.MSG: God said to Cain, "Where is Abel your brother?"He said, "How should I know? Am I his babysitter?"
1_4_9.NIV: Then the Lord said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?”“I don’t know,” he replied. “Am I my brother’s keeper?”
1_4_9.NRSVCE: Then the LORD said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?” He said, “I do not know; am I my brother’s keeper?”

1_4_10.NKJV: And He said, “What have you done? The voice of your brother’s blood cries out to Me from the ground.
1_4_10.KJV: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth unto me from the ground. blood: Heb. bloods
1_4_10.ESV: And the LORD said, “What have you done? The voice of your brother's blood is crying to me from the ground.
1_4_10.NASB: He said, 'What have you done? The voice of your brother's blood is crying to Me from the ground.
1_4_10.NLT: But the LORD said, “What have you done? Listen! Your brother’s blood cries out to me from the ground!
1_4_10.WEB: Yahweh said, “What have you done? The voice of your brother’s blood cries to me from the ground.
1_4_10.CSB17: Then he said, “What have you done? Your brother’s blood cries out to me from the ground!
1_4_10.AMP: The LORD said, "What have you done? The voice of your brother's [innocent] blood is crying out to Me from the ground [for justice].
1_4_10.MSG: God said, "What have you done! The voice of your brother's blood is calling to me from the ground.
1_4_10.NIV: The Lord said, “What have you done? Listen! Your brother’s blood cries out to me from the ground.
1_4_10.NRSVCE: And the LORD said, “What have you done? Listen; your brother’s blood is crying out to me from the ground!

1_4_11.NKJV: So now you are cursed from the earth, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
1_4_11.KJV: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand;
1_4_11.ESV: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
1_4_11.NASB: 'Now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
1_4_11.NLT: Now you are cursed and banished from the ground, which has swallowed your brother’s blood.
1_4_11.WEB: Now you are cursed because of the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
1_4_11.CSB17: So now you are cursed, alienated from the ground that opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood you have shed.
1_4_11.AMP: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's [shed] blood from your hand.
1_4_11.MSG: From now on you'll get nothing but curses from this ground; you'll be driven from this ground that has opened its arms to receive the blood of your murdered brother.
1_4_11.NIV: Now you are under a curse and driven from the ground, which opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
1_4_11.NRSVCE: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.

1_4_12.NKJV: When you till the ground, it shall no longer yield its strength to you. A fugitive and a vagabond you shall be on the earth.”
1_4_12.KJV: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
1_4_12.ESV: When you work the ground, it shall no longer yield to you its strength. You shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.”
1_4_12.NASB: 'When you cultivate the ground, it will no longer yield its strength to you; you will be a vagrant and a wanderer on the earth.'
1_4_12.NLT: No longer will the ground yield good crops for you, no matter how hard you work! From now on you will be a homeless wanderer on the earth.”
1_4_12.WEB: From now on, when you till the ground, it won’t yield its strength to you. You will be a fugitive and a wanderer in the earth.”
1_4_12.CSB17: If you work the ground, it will never again give you its yield. You will be a restless wanderer on the earth.”
1_4_12.AMP: When you cultivate the ground, it shall no longer yield its strength [it will resist producing good crops] for you; you shall be a fugitive and a vagabond [roaming aimlessly] on the earth [in perpetual exile without a home, a degraded outcast]."
1_4_12.MSG: You'll farm this ground, but it will no longer give you its best. You'll be a homeless wanderer on Earth."
1_4_12.NIV: When you work the ground, it will no longer yield its crops for you. You will be a restless wanderer on the earth.”
1_4_12.NRSVCE: When you till the ground, it will no longer yield to you its strength; you will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.”

1_4_13.NKJV: And Cain said to the LORD, “My punishment is greater than I can bear!

1_4_13.ESV: Cain said to the LORD, “My punishment is greater than I can bear.
1_4_13.NASB: Cain said to the LORD, 'My punishment is too great to bear!
1_4_13.NLT: Cain replied to the LORD, “My punishment is too great for me to bear!

1_4_13.NIV: Cain said to the Lord, “My punishment is more than I can bear.
1_4_13.NRSVCE: Cain said to the LORD, “My punishment is greater than I can bear!

1_4_14.NKJV: Surely You have driven me out this day from the face of the ground; I shall be hidden from Your face; I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond on the earth, and it will happen that anyone who finds me will kill me.”
1_4_14.KJV: Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that every one that findeth me shall slay me.
1_4_14.ESV: Behold, you have driven me today away from the ground, and from your face I shall be hidden. I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me.”
1_4_14.NASB: 'Behold, You have driven me this day from the face of the ground; and from Your face I will be hidden, and I will be a vagrant and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me.'
1_4_14.NLT: You have banished me from the land and from your presence; you have made me a homeless wanderer. Anyone who finds me will kill me!”
1_4_14.WEB: Behold, you have driven me out today from the surface of the ground. I will be hidden from your face, and I will be a fugitive and a wanderer in the earth. Whoever finds me will kill me.”
1_4_14.CSB17: Since you are banishing me today from the face of the earth, and I must hide from your presence and become a restless wanderer on the earth, whoever finds me will kill me.”
1_4_14.AMP: Behold, You have driven me out this day from the face of the land; and from Your face (presence) I will be hidden, and I will be a fugitive and an [aimless] vagabond on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me."
1_4_14.MSG: You've thrown me off the land and I can never again face you. I'm a homeless wanderer on Earth and whoever finds me will kill me."
1_4_14.NIV: Today you are driving me from the land, and I will be hidden from your presence; I will be a restless wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me.”
1_4_14.NRSVCE: Today you have driven me away from the soil, and I shall be hidden from your face; I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and anyone who meets me may kill me.”

1_4_15.NKJV: And the LORD said to him, “Therefore, whoever kills Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold.” And the LORD set a mark on Cain, lest anyone finding him should kill him.
1_4_15.KJV: And the LORD said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the LORD set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.
1_4_15.ESV: Then the LORD said to him, “Not so! If anyone kills Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold.” And the LORD put a mark on Cain, lest any who found him should attack him.
1_4_15.NASB: So the LORD said to him, 'Therefore whoever kills Cain, vengeance will be taken on him sevenfold.' And the LORD appointed a sign for Cain, so that no one finding him would slay him.
1_4_15.NLT: The LORD replied, “No, for I will give a sevenfold punishment to anyone who kills you.” Then the LORD put a mark on Cain to warn anyone who might try to kill him.
1_4_15.WEB: Yahweh said to him, “Therefore whoever slays Cain, vengeance will be taken on him sevenfold.” Yahweh appointed a sign for Cain, so that anyone finding him would not strike him.
1_4_15.CSB17: Then the LORD replied to him, “In that case, whoever kills Cain will suffer vengeance seven times over.” And he placed a mark on Cain so that whoever found him would not kill him.
1_4_15.AMP: And the LORD said to him, " Therefore, whoever kills Cain, a sevenfold vengeance [that is, punishment seven times worse] shall be taken on him [by Me]." And the LORD set a [protective] mark (sign) on Cain, so that no one who found (met) him would kill him.
1_4_15.MSG: God told him, "No. Anyone who kills Cain will pay for it seven times over." God put a mark on Cain to protect him so that no one who met him would kill him.
1_4_15.NIV: But the Lord said to him, “Not so; if anyone kills Cain, he will suffer vengeance seven times over.” Then the Lord put a mark on Cain so that no one who found him would kill him.
1_4_15.NRSVCE: Then the LORD said to him, “Not so! Whoever kills Cain will suffer a sevenfold vengeance.” And the LORD put a mark on Cain, so that no one who came upon him would kill him.

1_4_16.NKJV: Then Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and dwelt in the land of Nod on the east of Eden.

1_4_17.NKJV: And Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Ē´noch. And he built a city, and called the name of the city after the name of his son—Ē´noch.

1_4_25.NKJV: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, “For God has appointed another seed for me instead of Abel, whom Cain killed.”
1_4_25.KJV: And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew. Seth: Heb. Sheth: that is Appointed, or, Put

1_4_25.NASB: Adam had relations with his wife again; and she gave birth to a son, and named him Seth, for, [she said], 'God has appointed me another offspring in place of Abel, for Cain killed him.'

1_4_25.NRSVCE: Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, for she said, “God has appointed for me another child instead of Abel, because Cain killed him.”

1_4_26.NKJV: And as for Seth, to him also a son was born; and he named him Ē´nosh. Then men began to call on the name of the LORD.
1_4_26.KJV: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos: then began men to call upon the name of the LORD. Enos: Heb. Enosh to call: or, to call themselves by the name of the Lord
1_4_26.ESV: To Seth also a son was born, and he called his name Enosh. At that time people began to call upon the name of the LORD.
1_4_26.NASB: To Seth, to him also a son was born; and he called his name Enosh. Then [men] began to call upon the name of the LORD.
1_4_26.NLT: When Seth grew up, he had a son and named him Enosh. At that time people first began to worship the LORD by name.
1_4_26.WEB: A son was also born to Seth, and he named him Enosh. At that time men began to call on Yahweh’s name.
1_4_26.CSB17: A son was born to Seth also, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to call on the name of the LORD.
1_4_26.AMP: To Seth, also, a son was born, whom he named Enosh (mortal man, mankind). At that [same] time men began to call on the name of the LORD [in worship through prayer, praise, and thanksgiving].
1_4_26.MSG: And then Seth had a son whom he named Enosh.That's when men and women began praying and worshiping in the name of God.
1_4_26.NIV: Seth also had a son, and he named him Enosh.At that time men began to call on the name of the Lord.
1_4_26.NRSVCE: To Seth also a son was born, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to invoke the name of the LORD.

1_5_1.NKJV: This is the book of the genealogy of Adam. In the day that God created man, He made him in the likeness of God.

1_5_1.CSB17: This is the document containing the family records of Adam. On the day that God created man, he made him in the likeness of God;
1_5_1.AMP: This is the book (the written record, the history) of the generations of [the descendants of] Adam. When God created man, He made him in the likeness of God [not physical, but a spiritual personality and moral likeness].
1_5_1.MSG: This is the family tree of the human race: When God created the human race, he made it godlike, with a nature akin to God.

1_6_3.NKJV: And the LORD said, “My Spirit shall not strive with man forever, for he is indeed flesh; yet his days shall be one hundred and twenty years.”
1_6_3.KJV: And the LORD said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh : yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.
1_6_3.ESV: Then the LORD said, “My Spirit shall not abide in man forever, for he is flesh: his days shall be 120 years.”
1_6_3.NASB: Then the LORD said, 'My Spirit shall not strive with man forever, because he also is flesh; nevertheless his days shall be one hundred and twenty years.'
1_6_3.NLT: Then the LORD said, “My Spirit will not put up with humans for such a long time, for they are only mortal flesh. In the future, their normal lifespan will be no more than 120 years.”
1_6_3.WEB: Yahweh said, “My Spirit will not strive with man forever, because he also is flesh; so his days will be one hundred twenty years.”
1_6_3.CSB17: And the LORD said, “My Spirit will not remain with mankind forever, because they are corrupt. Their days will be 120 years.”
1_6_3.AMP: Then the LORD said, "My Spirit shall not strive and remain with man forever, because he is indeed flesh [sinful, corrupt—given over to sensual appetites]; nevertheless his days shall yet be a hundred and twenty years."
1_6_3.MSG: Then God said, "I'm not going to breathe life into men and women endlessly. Eventually they're going to die; from now on they can expect a life span of 120 years."
1_6_3.NIV: Then the Lord said, “My Spirit will not contend with man forever, for he is mortal; his days will be a hundred and twenty years.”
1_6_3.NRSVCE: Then the LORD said, “My spirit shall not abide in mortals forever, for they are flesh; their days shall be one hundred twenty years.”

1_6_4.NKJV: There were giants on the earth in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of God came in to the daughters of men and they bore children to them. Those were the mighty men who were of old, men of renown.

1_6_4.MSG: This was back in the days (and also later) when there were giants in the land. The giants came from the union of the sons of God and the daughters of men. These were the mighty men of ancient lore, the famous ones.

1_6_7.NKJV: So the LORD said, “I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth, both man and beast, creeping thing and birds of the air, for I am sorry that I have made them.”

1_6_11.CSB17: Now the earth was corrupt in God’s sight, and the earth was filled with wickedness.
1_6_11.AMP: The [population of the] earth was corrupt [absolutely depraved—spiritually and morally putrid] in God's sight, and the land was filled with violence [desecration, infringement, outrage, assault, and lust for power].
1_6_11.MSG: As far as God was concerned, the Earth had become a sewer; there was violence everywhere.
1_6_11.NIV: Now the earth was corrupt in God’s sight and was full of violence.
1_6_11.NRSVCE: Now the earth was corrupt in God’s sight, and the earth was filled with violence.

1_6_12.NKJV: So God looked upon the earth, and indeed it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted their way on the earth.
1_6_12.KJV: And God looked upon the earth, and, behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth.
1_6_12.ESV: And God saw the earth, and behold, it was corrupt, for all flesh had corrupted their way on the earth.
1_6_12.NASB: God looked on the earth, and behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted their way upon the earth.
1_6_12.NLT: God observed all this corruption in the world, for everyone on earth was corrupt.
1_6_12.WEB: God saw the earth, and saw that it was corrupt, for all flesh had corrupted their way on the earth.
1_6_12.CSB17: God saw how corrupt the earth was, for every creature had corrupted its way on the earth.
1_6_12.AMP: God looked on the earth and saw how debased and degenerate it was, for all humanity had corrupted their way on the earth and lost their true direction.
1_6_12.MSG: God took one look and saw how bad it was, everyone corrupt and corrupting—life itself corrupt to the core.
1_6_12.NIV: God saw how corrupt the earth had become, for all the people on earth had corrupted their ways.
1_6_12.NRSVCE: And God saw that the earth was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted its ways upon the earth.

1_6_13.NKJV: And God said to Noah, “The end of all flesh has come before Me, for the earth is filled with violence through them; and behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
1_6_13.KJV: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth. with the earth: or, from the earth
1_6_13.ESV: And God said to Noah, “I have determined to make an end of all flesh, for the earth is filled with violence through them. Behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
1_6_13.NASB: Then God said to Noah, 'The end of all flesh has come before Me; for the earth is filled with violence because of them; and behold, I am about to destroy them with the earth.
1_6_13.NLT: So God said to Noah, “I have decided to destroy all living creatures, for they have filled the earth with violence. Yes, I will wipe them all out along with the earth!
1_6_13.WEB: God said to Noah, “I will bring an end to all flesh, for the earth is filled with violence through them. Behold, I will destroy them and the earth.
1_6_13.CSB17: Then God said to Noah, “I have decided to put an end to every creature, for the earth is filled with wickedness because of them; therefore I am going to destroy them along with the earth.
1_6_13.AMP: God said to Noah, "I intend to make an end of all that lives, for through men the land is filled with violence; and behold, I am about to destroy them together with the land.
1_6_13.MSG: God said to Noah, "It's all over. It's the end of the human race. The violence is everywhere; I'm making a clean sweep.
1_6_13.NIV: So God said to Noah, “I am going to put an end to all people, for the earth is filled with violence because of them. I am surely going to destroy both them and the earth.
1_6_13.NRSVCE: And God said to Noah, “I have determined to make an end of all flesh, for the earth is filled with violence because of them; now I am going to destroy them along with the earth.

1_6_14.NKJV: Make yourself an ark of gopherwood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and outside with pitch.
1_6_14.KJV: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch. rooms: Heb. nests
1_6_14.ESV: Make yourself an ark of gopher wood. Make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and out with pitch.
1_6_14.NASB: 'Make for yourself an ark of gopher wood; you shall make the ark with rooms, and shall cover it inside and out with pitch.
1_6_14.NLT: “Build a large boat from cypress wood and waterproof it with tar, inside and out. Then construct decks and stalls throughout its interior.
1_6_14.WEB: Make a ship of gopher wood. You shall make rooms in the ship, and shall seal it inside and outside with pitch.
1_6_14.CSB17: “Make yourself an ark of gopher wood. Make rooms in the ark, and cover it with pitch inside and outside.
1_6_14.AMP: Make yourself an ark of gopher wood; make in it rooms (stalls, pens, coops, nests, cages, compartments) and coat it inside and out with pitch (bitumen).
1_6_14.MSG: "Build yourself a ship from teakwood. Make rooms in it. Coat it with pitch inside and out.
1_6_14.NIV: So make yourself an ark of cypress wood; make rooms in it and coat it with pitch inside and out.
1_6_14.NRSVCE: Make yourself an ark of cypress wood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and out with pitch.

1_6_15.NKJV: And this is how you shall make it: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
1_6_15.KJV: And this is the fashion which thou shalt make it of: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.
1_6_15.ESV: This is how you are to make it: the length of the ark 300 cubits, its breadth 50 cubits, and its height 30 cubits.
1_6_15.NASB: 'This is how you shall make it: the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its breadth fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
1_6_15.NLT: Make the boat 450 feet long, 75 feet wide, and 45 feet high.

1_6_15.CSB17: This is how you are to make it: The ark will be 450 feet long, 75 feet wide, and 45 feet high.
1_6_15.AMP: This is the way you are to make it: the length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits (450' x 75' x 45').
1_6_15.MSG: Make it 450 feet long, seventy-five feet wide, and forty-five feet high.
1_6_15.NIV: This is how you are to build it: The ark is to be 450 feet long, 75 feet wide and 45 feet high.
1_6_15.NRSVCE: This is how you are to make it: the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.

1_6_16.NKJV: You shall make a window for the ark, and you shall finish it to a cubit from above; and set the door of the ark in its side. You shall make it with lower, second, and third decks.
1_6_16.KJV: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
1_6_16.ESV: Make a roof for the ark, and finish it to a cubit above, and set the door of the ark in its side. Make it with lower, second, and third decks.
1_6_16.NASB: 'You shall make a window for the ark, and finish it to a cubit from the top; and set the door of the ark in the side of it; you shall make it with lower, second, and third decks.
1_6_16.NLT: Leave an 18-inch opening below the roof all the way around the boat. Put the door on the side, and build three decks inside the boat — lower, middle, and upper.
1_6_16.WEB: You shall make a roof in the ship, and you shall finish it to a cubit upward. You shall set the door of the ship in its side. You shall make it with lower, second, and third levels.
1_6_16.CSB17: You are to make a roof, finishing the sides of the ark to within eighteen inches of the roof. You are to put a door in the side of the ark. Make it with lower, middle, and upper decks.
1_6_16.AMP: You shall make a window [for light and ventilation] for the ark, and finish it to at least a cubit (eighteen inches) from the top—and set the [entry] door of the ark in its side; and you shall make it with lower, second and third decks.
1_6_16.MSG: Build a roof for it and put in a window eighteen inches from the top; put in a door on the side of the ship; and make three decks, lower, middle, and upper.
1_6_16.NIV: Make a roof for it and finish the ark to within 18 inches of the top. Put a door in the side of the ark and make lower, middle and upper decks.
1_6_16.NRSVCE: Make a roof for the ark, and finish it to a cubit above; and put the door of the ark in its side; make it with lower, second, and third decks.

1_6_17.NKJV: And behold, I Myself am bringing floodwaters on the earth, to destroy from under heaven all flesh in which is the breath of life; everything that is on the earth shall die.
1_6_17.KJV: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.

1_6_17.NASB: 'Behold, I, even I am bringing the flood of water upon the earth, to destroy all flesh in which is the breath of life, from under heaven; everything that is on the earth shall perish.
1_6_17.NLT: “Look! I am about to cover the earth with a flood that will destroy every living thing that breathes. Everything on earth will die.
1_6_17.WEB: I, even I, do bring the flood of waters on this earth, to destroy all flesh having the breath of life from under the sky. Everything that is in the earth will die.
1_6_17.CSB17: “Understand that I am bringing a flood — floodwaters on the earth to destroy every creature under heaven with the breath of life in it. Everything on earth will perish.
1_6_17.AMP: For behold, I, even I, will bring a flood of waters on the earth, to destroy all life under the heavens in which there is the breath and spirit of life; everything that is on the land shall die.
1_6_17.MSG: "I'm going to bring a flood on the Earth that will destroy everything alive under Heaven. Total destruction.
1_6_17.NIV: I am going to bring floodwaters on the earth to destroy all life under the heavens, every creature that has the breath of life in it. Everything on earth will perish.
1_6_17.NRSVCE: For my part, I am going to bring a flood of waters on the earth, to destroy from under heaven all flesh in which is the breath of life; everything that is on the earth shall die.

1_6_18.NKJV: But I will establish My covenant with you; and you shall go into the ark—you, your sons, your wife, and your sons’ wives with you.

1_6_18.NRSVCE: But I will establish my covenant with you; and you shall come into the ark, you, your sons, your wife, and your sons’ wives with you.

1_6_19.NKJV: And of every living thing of all flesh you shall bring two of every sort into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.

1_6_19.NRSVCE: And of every living thing, of all flesh, you shall bring two of every kind into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.

1_6_20.NRSVCE: Of the birds according to their kinds, and of the animals according to their kinds, of every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of every kind shall come in to you, to keep them alive.

1_7_3.NRSVCE: and seven pairs of the birds of the air also, male and female, to keep their kind alive on the face of all the earth.

1_8_21.NKJV: And the LORD smelled a soothing aroma. Then the LORD said in His heart, “I will never again curse the ground for man’s sake, although the imagination of man’s heart is evil from his youth; nor will I again destroy every living thing as I have done.

1_8_21.ESV: And when the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, the LORD said in his heart, “I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the intention of man's heart is evil from his youth. Neither will I ever again strike down every living creature as I have done.
1_8_21.NASB: The LORD smelled the soothing aroma; and the LORD said to Himself, 'I will never again curse the ground on account of man, for the intent of man's heart is evil from his youth; and I will never again destroy every living thing, as I have done.
1_8_21.NLT: And the LORD was pleased with the aroma of the sacrifice and said to himself, “I will never again curse the ground because of the human race, even though everything they think or imagine is bent toward evil from childhood. I will never again destroy all living things.
1_8_21.WEB: Yahweh smelled the pleasant aroma. Yahweh said in his heart, “I will not again curse the ground any more for man’s sake because the imagination of man’s heart is evil from his youth. I will never again strike every living thing, as I have done.
1_8_21.CSB17: When the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, he said to himself, “I will never again curse the ground because of human beings, even though the inclination of the human heart is evil from youth onward. And I will never again strike down every living thing as I have done.
1_8_21.AMP: The LORD smelled the pleasing aroma [a soothing, satisfying scent] and the LORD said to Himself, "I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the intent (strong inclination, desire) of man's heart is wicked from his youth; and I will never again destroy every living thing, as I have done.
1_8_21.MSG: God smelled the sweet fragrance and thought to himself, "I'll never again curse the ground because of people. I know they have this bent toward evil from an early age, but I'll never again kill off everything living as I've just done.
1_8_21.NIV: The Lord smelled the pleasing aroma and said in his heart: “Never again will I curse the ground because of man, even though every inclination of his heart is evil from childhood. And never again will I destroy all living creatures, as I have done.

1_10_9.NKJV: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod the mighty hunter before the LORD.”
1_10_9.KJV: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD: wherefore it is said, Even as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the LORD.
1_10_9.ESV: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD. Therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the LORD.”
1_10_9.NASB: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it is said, 'Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the LORD.'
1_10_9.NLT: Since he was the greatest hunter in the world, his name became proverbial. People would say, “This man is like Nimrod, the greatest hunter in the world.”
1_10_9.WEB: He was a mighty hunter before Yahweh. Therefore it is said, “like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before Yahweh”.
1_10_9.CSB17: He was a powerful hunter in the sight of the LORD. That is why it is said, “Like Nimrod, a powerful hunter in the sight of the LORD.”
1_10_9.AMP: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it is said, "Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the LORD."
1_10_9.MSG: He was a great hunter before God. There was a saying, "Like Nimrod, a great hunter before God."
1_10_9.NIV: He was a mighty hunter before the Lord; that is why it is said, “Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the Lord.”
1_10_9.NRSVCE: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the LORD.”

1_10_10.NKJV: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
1_10_10.KJV: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar. Babel: Gr. Babylon

1_10_10.NLT: He built his kingdom in the land of Babylonia, with the cities of Babylon, Erech, Akkad, and Calneh.
1_10_10.WEB: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
1_10_10.CSB17: His kingdom started with Babylon, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
1_10_10.AMP: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel and Erech and Accad and Calneh, in the land of Shinar [in Babylonia].
1_10_10.MSG: His kingdom got its start with Babel; then Erech, Akkad, and Calneh in the country of Shinar.
1_10_10.NIV: The first centers of his kingdom were Babylon, Erech, Akkad and Calneh, in Shinar.
1_10_10.NRSVCE: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, and Accad, all of them in the land of Shinar.

1_10_31.CSB17: These are Shem’s sons by their clans, according to their languages, in their lands and their nations.
1_10_31.AMP: These are Shem's descendants according to their constituent groups (families), according to their languages, by their lands, according to their nations.
1_10_31.MSG: These are the descendants of Shem by family, language, country, and nation.
1_10_31.NIV: These are the sons of Shem by their clans and languages, in their territories and nations.
1_10_31.NRSVCE: These are the descendants of Shem, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.

1_10_32.NKJV: These were the families of the sons of Noah, according to their generations, in their nations; and from these the nations were divided on the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.KJV: These are the families of the sons of Noah, after their generations, in their nations: and by these were the nations divided in the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.ESV: These are the clans of the sons of Noah, according to their genealogies, in their nations, and from these the nations spread abroad on the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.NASB: These are the families of the sons of Noah, according to their genealogies, by their nations; and out of these the nations were separated on the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.NLT: These are the clans that descended from Noah’s sons, arranged by nation according to their lines of descent. All the nations of the earth descended from these clans after the great flood.
1_10_32.WEB: These are the families of the sons of Noah, by their generations, according to their nations. The nations divided from these in the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.CSB17: These are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their family records, in their nations. The nations on earth spread out from these after the flood.
1_10_32.AMP: These are the families of the sons of Noah, according to their descendants, by their nations; and from these [people] the nations were separated and spread abroad on the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.MSG: This is the family tree of the sons of Noah as they developed into nations. From them nations developed all across the Earth after the flood.
1_10_32.NIV: These are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their lines of descent, within their nations. From these the nations spread out over the earth after the flood.
1_10_32.NRSVCE: These are the families of Noah’s sons, according to their genealogies, in their nations; and from these the nations spread abroad on the earth after the flood.

1_11_1.NKJV: Now the whole earth had one language and one speech.
1_11_1.KJV: And the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech. language: Heb. lip. speech: Heb. words
1_11_1.ESV: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
1_11_1.NASB: Now the whole earth used the same language and the same words.
1_11_1.NLT: At one time all the people of the world spoke the same language and used the same words.
1_11_1.WEB: The whole earth was of one language and of one speech.
1_11_1.CSB17: The whole earth had the same language and vocabulary.
1_11_1.AMP: Now the whole earth spoke one language and used the same words (vocabulary).
1_11_1.MSG: At one time, the whole Earth spoke the same language.
1_11_1.NIV: The Tower of BabelNow the whole world had one language and a common speech.
1_11_1.NRSVCE: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.

1_11_2.NKJV: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar, and they dwelt there.
1_11_2.KJV: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there. from: or, eastward
1_11_2.ESV: And as people migrated from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
1_11_2.NASB: It came about as they journeyed east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
1_11_2.NLT: As the people migrated to the east, they found a plain in the land of Babylonia and settled there.
1_11_2.WEB: As they traveled east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar, and they lived there.
1_11_2.CSB17: As people migrated from the east, they found a valley in the land of Shinar and settled there.
1_11_2.AMP: And as people journeyed eastward, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and they settled there.
1_11_2.MSG: It so happened that as they moved out of the east, they came upon a plain in the land of Shinar and settled down.
1_11_2.NIV: As men moved eastward, they found a plain in Shinar and settled there.
1_11_2.NRSVCE: And as they migrated from the east, they came upon a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.

1_11_3.NKJV: Then they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks and bake them thoroughly.” They had brick for stone, and they had asphalt for mortar.
1_11_3.KJV: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly. And they had brick for stone, and slime had they for morter. they said: Heb. a man said to his neighbour burn: Heb. burn them to a burning
1_11_3.ESV: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
1_11_3.NASB: They said to one another, 'Come, let us make bricks and burn [them] thoroughly.' And they used brick for stone, and they used tar for mortar.
1_11_3.NLT: They began saying to each other, “Let’s make bricks and harden them with fire.” (In this region bricks were used instead of stone, and tar was used for mortar.)
1_11_3.WEB: They said to one another, “Come, let’s make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” They had brick for stone, and they used tar for mortar.
1_11_3.CSB17: They said to each other, “Come, let us make oven-fired bricks.” (They used brick for stone and asphalt for mortar.)
1_11_3.AMP: They said one to another, "Come, let us make bricks and fire them thoroughly [in a kiln, to harden and strengthen them]." So they used brick for stone [as building material], and they used tar (bitumen, asphalt) for mortar.
1_11_3.MSG: They said to one another, "Come, let's make bricks and fire them well." They used brick for stone and tar for mortar.
1_11_3.NIV: They said to each other, “Come, let’s make bricks and bake them thoroughly.” They used brick instead of stone, and tar for mortar.
1_11_3.NRSVCE: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.

1_11_4.NKJV: And they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, and a tower whose top is in the heavens; let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered abroad over the face of the whole earth.”
1_11_4.KJV: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven; and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.
1_11_4.ESV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be dispersed over the face of the whole earth.”
1_11_4.NASB: They said, 'Come, let us build for ourselves a city, and a tower whose top [will reach] into heaven, and let us make for ourselves a name, otherwise we will be scattered abroad over the face of the whole earth.'
1_11_4.NLT: Then they said, “Come, let’s build a great city for ourselves with a tower that reaches into the sky. This will make us famous and keep us from being scattered all over the world.”
1_11_4.WEB: They said, “Come, let’s build ourselves a city, and a tower whose top reaches to the sky, and let’s make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered abroad on the surface of the whole earth.”
1_11_4.CSB17: And they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the sky. Let us make a name for ourselves; otherwise, we will be scattered throughout the earth.”
1_11_4.AMP: They said, "Come, let us build a city for ourselves, and a tower whose top will reach into the heavens, and let us make a [famous] name for ourselves, so that we will not be scattered [into separate groups] and be dispersed over the surface of the entire earth [as the LORD instructed]."
1_11_4.MSG: Then they said, "Come, let's build ourselves a city and a tower that reaches Heaven. Let's make ourselves famous so we won't be scattered here and there across the Earth."
1_11_4.NIV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, with a tower that reaches to the heavens, so that we may make a name for ourselves and not be scattered over the face of the whole earth.”
1_11_4.NRSVCE: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves; otherwise we shall be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.”

1_11_5.NKJV: But the LORD came down to see the city and the tower which the sons of men had built.
1_11_5.KJV: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.
1_11_5.ESV: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of man had built.
1_11_5.NASB: The LORD came down to see the city and the tower which the sons of men had built.

1_11_5.WEB: Yahweh came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men built.
1_11_5.CSB17: Then the LORD came down to look over the city and the tower that the humans were building.
1_11_5.AMP: Now the LORD came down to see the city and the tower which the sons of men had built.
1_11_5.MSG: God came down to look over the city and the tower those people had built.
1_11_5.NIV: But the Lord came down to see the city and the tower that the men were building.
1_11_5.NRSVCE: The LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which mortals had built.

1_11_6.NKJV: And the LORD said, “Indeed the people are one and they all have one language, and this is what they begin to do; now nothing that they propose to do will be withheld from them.
1_11_6.KJV: And the LORD said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language; and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.
1_11_6.ESV: And the LORD said, “Behold, they are one people, and they have all one language, and this is only the beginning of what they will do. And nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
1_11_6.NASB: The LORD said, 'Behold, they are one people, and they all have the same language. And this is what they began to do, and now nothing which they purpose to do will be impossible for them.
1_11_6.NLT: “Look!” he said. “The people are united, and they all speak the same language. After this, nothing they set out to do will be impossible for them!
1_11_6.WEB: Yahweh said, “Behold, they are one people, and they have all one language, and this is what they begin to do. Now nothing will be withheld from them, which they intend to do.
1_11_6.CSB17: The LORD said, “If they have begun to do this as one people all having the same language, then nothing they plan to do will be impossible for them.
1_11_6.AMP: And the LORD said, "Behold, they are one [unified] people, and they all have the same language. This is only the beginning of what they will do [in rebellion against Me], and now no evil thing they imagine they can do will be impossible for them.
1_11_6.MSG: God took one look and said, "One people, one language; why, this is only a first step. No telling what they'll come up with next—they'll stop at nothing!
1_11_6.NIV: The Lord said, “If as one people speaking the same language they have begun to do this, then nothing they plan to do will be impossible for them.
1_11_6.NRSVCE: And the LORD said, “Look, they are one people, and they have all one language; and this is only the beginning of what they will do; nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.

1_11_7.NKJV: Come, let Us go down and there confuse their language, that they may not understand one another’s speech.”

1_11_8.NKJV: So the LORD scattered them abroad from there over the face of all the earth, and they ceased building the city.

1_11_9.NKJV: Therefore its name is called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of all the earth; and from there the LORD scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
1_11_9.KJV: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the LORD did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the LORD scatter them abroad upon the face of all the earth. Babel: that is, Confusion
1_11_9.ESV: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of all the earth. And from there the LORD dispersed them over the face of all the earth.

1_11_31.NKJV: And Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram’s wife, and they went out with them from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan; and they came to Haran and dwelt there.
1_11_31.KJV: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son's son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram's wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.
1_11_31.ESV: Terah took Abram his son and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife, and they went forth together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan, but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
1_11_31.NASB: Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife; and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans in order to enter the land of Canaan; and they went as far as Haran, and settled there.
1_11_31.NLT: One day Terah took his son Abram, his daughter-in-law Sarai (his son Abram’s wife), and his grandson Lot (his son Haran’s child) and moved away from Ur of the Chaldeans. He was headed for the land of Canaan, but they stopped at Haran and settled there.
1_11_31.WEB: Terah took Abram his son, Lot the son of Haran, his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram’s wife. They went from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan. They came to Haran and lived there.
