1_11_31.AMP: Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife; and they went out together to go from Ur of the Chaldeans into the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran [about five hundred and fifty miles northwest of Ur], they settled there.
1_11_31.MSG: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot (Haran's son), and Sarai his daughter-in-law (his son Abram's wife) and set out with them from Ur of the Chaldees for the land of Canaan. But when they got as far as Haran, they settled down there.
1_11_31.NIV: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, the wife of his son Abram, and together they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to Canaan. But when they came to Haran, they settled there.

1_12_2.NKJV: I will make you a great nation; I will bless you And make your name great; And you shall be a blessing.

1_12_2.MSG: I'll make you a great nationand bless you.I'll make you famous;you'll be a blessing.
1_12_2.NIV: “I will make you into a great nationand I will bless you;I will make your name great,and you will be a blessing.
1_12_2.NRSVCE: I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you, and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.

1_12_3.NKJV: I will bless those who bless you, And I will curse him who curses you; And in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.”
1_12_3.KJV: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.
1_12_3.ESV: I will bless those who bless you, and him who dishonors you I will curse, and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.”
1_12_3.NASB: And I will bless those who bless you, And the one who curses you I will curse. And in you all the families of the earth will be blessed.'

1_12_3.AMP: And I will bless (do good for, benefit) those who bless you, And I will curse [that is, subject to My wrath and judgment] the one who curses (despises, dishonors, has contempt for) you. And in you all the families (nations) of the earth will be blessed."
1_12_3.MSG: I'll bless those who bless you;those who curse you I'll curse.All the families of the Earthwill be blessed through you."

1_12_3.NRSVCE: I will bless those who bless you, and the one who curses you I will curse; and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.”

1_14_7.NKJV: Then they turned back and came to En Mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and attacked all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who dwelt in Hazezon Tamar.

1_14_8.NKJV: And the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out and joined together in battle in the Valley of Siddim

1_14_10.NKJV: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of asphalt pits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled; some fell there, and the remainder fled to the mountains.
1_14_10.KJV: And the vale of Siddim was full of slimepits ; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there; and they that remained fled to the mountain.
1_14_10.ESV: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits, and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, and the rest fled to the hill country.
1_14_10.NASB: Now the valley of Siddim was full of tar pits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and they fell into them. But those who survived fled to the hill country.

1_14_11.NKJV: Then they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their provisions, and went their way.

1_14_12.NKJV: They also took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.

1_14_13.NKJV: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, for he dwelt by the terebinth trees of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner; and they were allies with Abram.
1_14_13.KJV: And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew; for he dwelt in the plain of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner: and these were confederate with Abram.
1_14_13.ESV: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and of Aner. These were allies of Abram.
1_14_13.NASB: Then a fugitive came and told Abram the Hebrew. Now he was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner, and these were allies with Abram.
1_14_13.NLT: But one of Lot’s men escaped and reported everything to Abram the Hebrew, who was living near the oak grove belonging to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his relatives, Eshcol and Aner, were Abram’s allies.
1_14_13.WEB: One who had escaped came and told Abram, the Hebrew. At that time, he lived by the oaks of Mamre, the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner; and they were allies of Abram.
1_14_13.CSB17: One of the survivors came and told Abram the Hebrew, who lived near the oaks belonging to Mamre the Amorite, the brother of Eshcol and the brother of Aner. They were bound by a treaty with Abram.
1_14_13.AMP: Then a survivor who had escaped [from the invading forces on the other side of the Jordan] came and told Abram the Hebrew. Now he was living by the terebinths (oaks) of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner—they were allies of Abram.
1_14_13.MSG: A fugitive came and reported to Abram the Hebrew. Abram was living at the Oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and Aner. They were allies of Abram.
1_14_13.NIV: One who had escaped came and reported this to Abram the Hebrew. Now Abram was living near the great trees of Mamre the Amorite, a brother of Eshcol and Aner, all of whom were allied with Abram.
1_14_13.NRSVCE: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and of Aner; these were allies of Abram.

1_14_14.NKJV: Now when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his three hundred and eighteen trained servants who were born in his own house, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
1_14_14.KJV: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his trained servants, born in his own house, three hundred and eighteen , and pursued them unto Dan. armed: or, led forth trained: or, instructed
1_14_14.ESV: When Abram heard that his kinsman had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, 318 of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
1_14_14.NASB: When Abram heard that his relative had been taken captive, he led out his trained men, born in his house, three hundred and eighteen, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
1_14_14.NLT: When Abram heard that his nephew Lot had been captured, he mobilized the 318 trained men who had been born into his household. Then he pursued Kedorlaomer’s army until he caught up with them at Dan.
1_14_14.WEB: When Abram heard that his relative was taken captive, he led out his trained men, born in his house, three hundred and eighteen, and pursued as far as Dan.
1_14_14.CSB17: When Abram heard that his relative had been taken prisoner, he assembled his 318 trained men, born in his household, and they went in pursuit as far as Dan.
1_14_14.AMP: When Abram heard that his nephew [Lot] had been captured, he armed and led out his trained men, born in his own house, [numbering] three hundred and eighteen, and went in pursuit as far [north] as Dan.
1_14_14.MSG: When Abram heard that his nephew had been taken prisoner, he lined up his servants, all of them born in his household—there were 318 of them—and chased after the captors all the way to Dan.
1_14_14.NIV: When Abram heard that his relative had been taken captive, he called out the 318 trained men born in his household and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
1_14_14.NRSVCE: When Abram heard that his nephew had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, three hundred eighteen of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.

1_14_15.NKJV: He divided his forces against them by night, and he and his servants attacked them and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is north of Damascus.
1_14_15.KJV: And he divided himself against them, he and his servants, by night, and smote them, and pursued them unto Hobah, which is on the left hand of Damascus.
1_14_15.ESV: And he divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and defeated them and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
1_14_15.NASB: He divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and defeated them, and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is north of Damascus.
1_14_15.NLT: There he divided his men and attacked during the night. Kedorlaomer’s army fled, but Abram chased them as far as Hobah, north of Damascus.
1_14_15.WEB: He divided himself against them by night, he and his servants, and struck them, and pursued them to Hobah, which is on the left hand of Damascus.
1_14_15.CSB17: And he and his servants deployed against them by night, defeated them, and pursued them as far as Hobah to the north of Damascus.
1_14_15.AMP: He divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and attacked and defeated them, and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is north of Damascus.
1_14_15.MSG: Abram and his men split into small groups and attacked by night. They chased them as far as Hobah, just north of Damascus.
1_14_15.NIV: During the night Abram divided his men to attack them and he routed them, pursuing them as far as Hobah, north of Damascus.
1_14_15.NRSVCE: He divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and routed them and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus.

1_14_16.NKJV: So he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his brother Lot and his goods, as well as the women and the people.

1_14_16.AMP: And he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his nephew Lot and his possessions, and also the women, and the people.
1_14_16.MSG: They recovered all the plunder along with nephew Lot and his possessions, including the women and the people.
1_14_16.NIV: He recovered all the goods and brought back his relative Lot and his possessions, together with the women and the other people.
1_14_16.NRSVCE: Then he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his nephew Lot with his goods, and the women and the people.

1_14_17.NKJV: And the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King’s Valley), after his return from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him.

1_16_2.NKJV: So Sarai said to Abram, “See now, the LORD has restrained me from bearing children. Please, go in to my maid; perhaps I shall obtain children by her.” And Abram heeded the voice of Sarai.
1_16_2.KJV: And Sarai said unto Abram, Behold now, the LORD hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee, go in unto my maid; it may be that I may obtain children by her. And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai. obtain: Heb. be built by her

1_16_2.NASB: So Sarai said to Abram, 'Now behold, the LORD has prevented me from bearing [children]. Please go in to my maid; perhaps I will obtain children through her.' And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.
1_16_2.NLT: So Sarai said to Abram, “The LORD has prevented me from having children. Go and sleep with my servant. Perhaps I can have children through her.” And Abram agreed with Sarai’s proposal.

1_16_2.MSG: Sarai said to Abram, "God has not seen fit to let me have a child. Sleep with my maid. Maybe I can get a family from her." Abram agreed to do what Sarai said.

1_16_5.NKJV: Then Sarai said to Abram, “My wrong be upon you! I gave my maid into your embrace; and when she saw that she had conceived, I became despised in her eyes. The LORD judge between you and me.”
1_16_5.KJV: And Sarai said unto Abram, My wrong be upon thee: I have given my maid into thy bosom; and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes: the LORD judge between me and thee.

1_16_5.NASB: And Sarai said to Abram, 'May the wrong done me be upon you. I gave my maid into your arms, but when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her sight. May the LORD judge between you and me.'
1_16_5.NLT: Then Sarai said to Abram, “This is all your fault! I put my servant into your arms, but now that she’s pregnant she treats me with contempt. The LORD will show who’s wrong — you or me!”
1_16_5.WEB: Sarai said to Abram, “This wrong is your fault. I gave my servant into your bosom, and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes. Yahweh judge between me and you.”
1_16_5.CSB17: Then Sarai said to Abram, “You are responsible for my suffering! I put my slave in your arms, and when she saw that she was pregnant, I became contemptible to her. May the LORD judge between me and you.”
1_16_5.AMP: Then Sarai said to Abram, "May [the responsibility for] the wrong done to me [by the arrogant behavior of Hagar] be upon you. I gave my maid into your arms, and when she realized that she had conceived, I was despised and looked on with disrespect. May the LORD judge [who has done right] between you and me."
1_16_5.MSG: Sarai told Abram, "It's all your fault that I'm suffering this abuse. I put my maid in bed with you and the minute she knows she's pregnant, she treats me like I'm nothing. May God decide which of us is right."
1_16_5.NIV: Then Sarai said to Abram, “You are responsible for the wrong I am suffering. I put my servant in your arms, and now that she knows she is pregnant, she despises me. May the Lord judge between you and me.”

1_16_6.MSG: "You decide," said Abram. "Your maid is your business."Sarai was abusive to Hagar and she ran away.

1_16_13.NKJV: Then she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her, You-Are-the-God-Who-Sees; for she said, “Have I also here seen Him who sees me?”

1_17_27.NKJV: and all the men of his house, born in the house or bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.

1_18_1.NKJV: Then the LORD appeared to him by the terebinth trees of Mamre, as he was sitting in the tent door in the heat of the day.
1_18_1.KJV: And the LORD appeared unto him in the plains of Mamre: and he sat in the tent door in the heat of the day;
1_18_1.ESV: And the LORD appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the door of his tent in the heat of the day.
1_18_1.NASB: Now the LORD appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, while he was sitting at the tent door in the heat of the day.
1_18_1.NLT: The LORD appeared again to Abraham near the oak grove belonging to Mamre. One day Abraham was sitting at the entrance to his tent during the hottest part of the day.
1_18_1.WEB: Yahweh appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat in the tent door in the heat of the day.
1_18_1.CSB17: The LORD appeared to Abraham at the oaks of Mamre while he was sitting at the entrance of his tent during the heat of the day.
1_18_1.AMP: Now the LORD appeared to Abraham by the terebinth trees of Mamre [in Hebron], while he was sitting at the tent door in the heat of the day.
1_18_1.MSG: God appeared to Abraham at the Oaks of Mamre while he was sitting at the entrance of his tent. It was the hottest part of the day.
1_18_1.NIV: The Three VisitorsThe Lord appeared to Abraham near the great trees of Mamre while he was sitting at the entrance to his tent in the heat of the day.
1_18_1.NRSVCE: The LORD appeared to Abraham by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the entrance of his tent in the heat of the day.

1_18_2.NKJV: So he lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing by him; and when he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them, and bowed himself to the ground,
1_18_2.KJV: And he lift up his eyes and looked, and, lo, three men stood by him: and when he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself toward the ground,
1_18_2.ESV: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing in front of him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed himself to the earth
1_18_2.NASB: When he lifted up his eyes and looked, behold, three men were standing opposite him; and when he saw [them], he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed himself to the earth,
1_18_2.NLT: He looked up and noticed three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he ran to meet them and welcomed them, bowing low to the ground.
1_18_2.WEB: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and saw that three men stood opposite him. When he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself to the earth,
1_18_2.CSB17: He looked up, and he saw three men standing near him. When he saw them, he ran from the entrance of the tent to meet them, bowed to the ground,
1_18_2.AMP: When he raised his eyes and looked up, behold, three men were standing [a little distance] from him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed down [with his face] to the ground,
1_18_2.MSG: He looked up and saw three men standing. He ran from his tent to greet them and bowed before them.
1_18_2.NIV: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he hurried from the entrance of his tent to meet them and bowed low to the ground.
1_18_2.NRSVCE: He looked up and saw three men standing near him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent entrance to meet them, and bowed down to the ground.

1_18_3.NKJV: and said, “My Lord, if I have now found favor in Your sight, do not pass on by Your servant.
1_18_3.KJV: And said, My Lord, if now I have found favour in thy sight, pass not away, I pray thee, from thy servant:
1_18_3.ESV: and said, “O Lord, if I have found favor in your sight, do not pass by your servant.
1_18_3.NASB: and said, 'My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass your servant by.
1_18_3.NLT: “My lord,” he said, “if it pleases you, stop here for a while.
1_18_3.WEB: and said, “My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, please don’t go away from your servant.
1_18_3.CSB17: and said, “My lord, if I have found favor with you, please do not go on past your servant.
1_18_3.AMP: and Abraham said, "My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass by your servant [without stopping to visit].
1_18_3.MSG: He said, "Master, if it please you, stop for a while with your servant.
1_18_3.NIV: He said, “If I have found favor in your eyes, my lord, do not pass your servant by.
1_18_3.NRSVCE: He said, “My lord, if I find favor with you, do not pass by your servant.

1_18_4.NKJV: Please let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.
1_18_4.KJV: Let a little water, I pray you, be fetched, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree:
1_18_4.ESV: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree,
1_18_4.NASB: 'Please let a little water be brought and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree;
1_18_4.NLT: Rest in the shade of this tree while water is brought to wash your feet.
1_18_4.WEB: Now let a little water be fetched, wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.
1_18_4.CSB17: Let a little water be brought, that you may wash your feet and rest yourselves under the tree.
1_18_4.AMP: Please let a little water be brought [by one of my servants] and [you may] wash your feet, and recline and rest comfortably under the tree.
1_18_4.MSG: I'll get some water so you can wash your feet. Rest under this tree.
1_18_4.NIV: Let a little water be brought, and then you may all wash your feet and rest under this tree.
1_18_4.NRSVCE: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.

1_18_5.NKJV: And I will bring a morsel of bread, that you may refresh your hearts. After that you may pass by, inasmuch as you have come to your servant.” They said, “Do as you have said.”
1_18_5.KJV: And I will fetch a morsel of bread, and comfort ye your hearts; after that ye shall pass on: for therefore are ye come to your servant. And they said, So do, as thou hast said. comfort: Heb. stay are: Heb. you have passed
1_18_5.ESV: while I bring a morsel of bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on — since you have come to your servant.” So they said, “Do as you have said.”
1_18_5.NASB: and I will bring a piece of bread, that you may refresh yourselves; after that you may go on, since you have visited your servant.' And they said, 'So do, as you have said.'
1_18_5.NLT: And since you’ve honored your servant with this visit, let me prepare some food to refresh you before you continue on your journey.” “All right,” they said. “Do as you have said.”
1_18_5.WEB: I will get a morsel of bread so you can refresh your heart. After that you may go your way, now that you have come to your servant.” They said, “Very well, do as you have said.”

1_18_5.MSG: I'll get some food to refresh you on your way, since your travels have brought you across my path."They said, "Certainly. Go ahead."
1_18_5.NIV: Let me get you something to eat, so you can be refreshed and then go on your way—now that you have come to your servant.”“Very well,” they answered, “do as you say.”
1_18_5.NRSVCE: Let me bring a little bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on — since you have come to your servant.” So they said, “Do as you have said.”

1_18_6.NKJV: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah and said, “Quickly, make ready three measures of fine meal; knead it and make cakes.”

1_18_6.NASB: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah, and said, 'Quickly, prepare three measures of fine flour, knead [it] and make bread cakes.'
1_18_6.NLT: So Abraham ran back to the tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get three large measures of your best flour, knead it into dough, and bake some bread.”

1_18_6.CSB17: So Abraham hurried into the tent and said to Sarah, “Quick! Knead three measures of fine flour and make bread.”
1_18_6.AMP: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah, and said, "Quickly, get ready three measures of fine meal, knead it and bake cakes."
1_18_6.MSG: Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah. He said, "Hurry. Get three cups of our best flour; knead it and make bread."

1_18_6.NRSVCE: And Abraham hastened into the tent to Sarah, and said, “Make ready quickly three measures of choice flour, knead it, and make cakes.”

1_18_7.NKJV: And Abraham ran to the herd, took a tender and good calf, gave it to a young man, and he hastened to prepare it.

1_18_7.NASB: Abraham also ran to the herd, and took a tender and choice calf and gave [it] to the servant, and he hurried to prepare it.
1_18_7.NLT: Then Abraham ran out to the herd and chose a tender calf and gave it to his servant, who quickly prepared it.

1_18_7.NRSVCE: Abraham ran to the herd, and took a calf, tender and good, and gave it to the servant, who hastened to prepare it.

1_18_9.NKJV: Then they said to him, “Where is Sarah your wife?” So he said, “Here, in the tent.”

1_18_9.CSB17: “Where is your wife Sarah?” they asked him. “There, in the tent,” he answered.

1_18_9.MSG: The men said to him, "Where is Sarah your wife?"He said, "In the tent."
1_18_9.NIV: “Where is your wife Sarah?” they asked him.“There, in the tent,” he said.
1_18_9.NRSVCE: They said to him, “Where is your wife Sarah?” And he said, “There, in the tent.”

1_18_10.NKJV: And He said, “I will certainly return to you according to the time of life, and behold, Sarah your wife shall have a son.” (Sarah was listening in the tent door which was behind him.)
1_18_10.KJV: And he said, I will certainly return unto thee according to the time of life; and, lo, Sarah thy wife shall have a son. And Sarah heard it in the tent door, which was behind him.

1_18_10.NASB: He said, 'I will surely return to you at this time next year; and behold, Sarah your wife will have a son.' And Sarah was listening at the tent door, which was behind him.

1_18_10.NIV: Then the Lord said, “I will surely return to you about this time next year, and Sarah your wife will have a son.”Now Sarah was listening at the entrance to the tent, which was behind him.
1_18_10.NRSVCE: Then one said, “I will surely return to you in due season, and your wife Sarah shall have a son.” And Sarah was listening at the tent entrance behind him.

1_18_11.NASB: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in age; Sarah was past childbearing.

1_18_11.NRSVCE: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in age; it had ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.

1_18_12.NKJV: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, “After I have grown old, shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?”
1_18_12.KJV: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, After I am waxed old shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?
1_18_12.ESV: So Sarah laughed to herself, saying, “After I am worn out, and my lord is old, shall I have pleasure?”
1_18_12.NASB: Sarah laughed to herself, saying, 'After I have become old, shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?'
1_18_12.NLT: So she laughed silently to herself and said, “How could a worn-out woman like me enjoy such pleasure, especially when my master — my husband — is also so old?”
1_18_12.WEB: Sarah laughed within herself, saying, “After I have grown old will I have pleasure, my lord being old also?”
1_18_12.CSB17: So she laughed to herself: “After I am worn out and my lord is old, will I have delight?”
1_18_12.AMP: So Sarah laughed to herself [when she heard the Lord's words], saying, "After I have become old, shall I have pleasure and delight, my lord (husband) being also old?"
1_18_12.MSG: Sarah laughed within herself, "An old woman like me? Get pregnant? With this old man of a husband?"

1_18_12.NRSVCE: So Sarah laughed to herself, saying, “After I have grown old, and my husband is old, shall I have pleasure?”

1_18_13.NKJV: And the LORD said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh, saying, ‘Shall I surely bear a child, since I am old?’

1_18_13.CSB17: But the LORD asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh, saying, ‘Can I really have a baby when I’m old?’

1_18_13.NIV: Then the Lord said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh and say, ‘Will I really have a child, now that I am old?’
1_18_13.NRSVCE: The LORD said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh, and say, ‘Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?’

1_18_14.NKJV: Is anything too hard for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall have a son.”
1_18_14.KJV: Is any thing too hard for the LORD? At the time appointed I will return unto thee, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall have a son.

1_18_14.NASB: 'Is anything too difficult for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, at this time next year, and Sarah will have a son.'
1_18_14.NLT: Is anything too hard for the LORD? I will return about this time next year, and Sarah will have a son.”
1_18_14.WEB: Is anything too hard for Yahweh? At the set time I will return to you, when the season comes round, and Sarah will have a son.”

1_18_14.AMP: Is anything too difficult or too wonderful for the LORD? At the appointed time, when the season [for her delivery] comes, I will return to you and Sarah will have a son."

1_18_14.NIV: Is anything too hard for the Lord? I will return to you at the appointed time next year and Sarah will have a son.”

1_18_15.NKJV: But Sarah denied it, saying, “I did not laugh,” for she was afraid. And He said, “No, but you did laugh!”

1_18_16.NKJV: Then the men rose from there and looked toward Sodom, and Abraham went with them to send them on the way.

1_19_15.NIV: With the coming of dawn, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Hurry! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or you will be swept away when the city is punished.”
1_19_15.NRSVCE: When morning dawned, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Get up, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or else you will be consumed in the punishment of the city.”

1_19_16.NKJV: And while he lingered, the men took hold of his hand, his wife’s hand, and the hands of his two daughters, the LORD being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.

1_19_16.ESV: But he lingered. So the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the LORD being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.
1_19_16.NASB: But he hesitated. So the men seized his hand and the hand of his wife and the hands of his two daughters, for the compassion of the LORD [was] upon him; and they brought him out, and put him outside the city.
1_19_16.NLT: When Lot still hesitated, the angels seized his hand and the hands of his wife and two daughters and rushed them to safety outside the city, for the LORD was merciful.
1_19_16.WEB: But he lingered; and the men grabbed his hand, his wife’s hand, and his two daughters’ hands, Yahweh being merciful to him; and they took him out, and set him outside of the city.

1_19_16.AMP: But Lot hesitated and lingered. The men took hold of his hand and the hand of his wife and the hands of his two daughters, because the LORD was merciful to him [for Abraham's sake]; and they brought him out, and left him outside the city [with his family].
1_19_16.MSG: Lot was dragging his feet. The men grabbed Lot's arm, and the arms of his wife and daughters—God was so merciful to them!—and dragged them to safety outside the city.

1_19_16.NRSVCE: But he lingered; so the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the LORD being merciful to him, and they brought him out and left him outside the city.

1_19_17.NKJV: So it came to pass, when they had brought them outside, that he said, “Escape for your life! Do not look behind you nor stay anywhere in the plain. Escape to the mountains, lest you be destroyed.”

1_19_19.NKJV: Indeed now, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have increased your mercy which you have shown me by saving my life; but I cannot escape to the mountains, lest some evil overtake me and I die.

1_20_13.NKJV: And it came to pass, when God caused me to wander from my father’s house, that I said to her, ‘This is your kindness that you should do for me: in every place, wherever we go, say of me, “He is my brother.” ’ ”

1_20_16.NKJV: Then to Sarah he said, “Behold, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; indeed this vindicates you before all who are with you and before everybody.” Thus she was rebuked.

1_21_12.NKJV: But God said to Abraham, “Do not let it be displeasing in your sight because of the lad or because of your bondwoman. Whatever Sarah has said to you, listen to her voice; for in Isaac your seed shall be called.

1_21_14.NKJV: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and took bread and a skin of water; and putting it on her shoulder, he gave it and the boy to Hagar, and sent her away. Then she departed and wandered in the Wilderness of Beersheba.

1_21_16.NKJV: Then she went and sat down across from him at a distance of about a bowshot; for she said to herself, “Let me not see the death of the boy.” So she sat opposite him, and lifted her voice and wept.

1_21_30.NKJV: And he said, “You will take these seven ewe lambs from my hand, that they may be my witness that I have dug this well.”

1_21_30.NLT: Abraham replied, “Please accept these seven lambs to show your agreement that I dug this well.”

1_21_30.NIV: He replied, “Accept these seven lambs from my hand as a witness that I dug this well.”

1_21_31.NKJV: Therefore he called that place Beersheba, because the two of them swore an oath there.

1_22_12.MSG: "Don't lay a hand on that boy! Don't touch him! Now I know how fearlessly you fear God; you didn't hesitate to place your son, your dear son, on the altar for me."

1_23_6.KJV: Hear us, my lord: thou art a mighty prince among us: in the choice of our sepulchres bury thy dead; none of us shall withhold from thee his sepulchre, but that thou mayest bury thy dead. a mighty: Heb. a prince of God
1_23_6.ESV: “Hear us, my lord; you are a prince of God among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will withhold from you his tomb to hinder you from burying your dead.”
1_23_6.NASB: 'Hear us, my lord, you are a mighty prince among us; bury your dead in the choicest of our graves; none of us will refuse you his grave for burying your dead.'

1_23_6.MSG: "Why, you're no mere outsider here with us, you're a prince of God! Bury your dead wife in the best of our burial sites. None of us will refuse you a place for burial."

1_24_23.NKJV: and said, “Whose daughter are you? Tell me, please, is there room in your father’s house for us to lodge?”

1_25_23.NASB: The LORD said to her, 'Two nations are in your womb; And two peoples will be separated from your body; And one people shall be stronger than the other; And the older shall serve the younger.'
1_25_23.NLT: And the LORD told her, “The sons in your womb will become two nations. From the very beginning, the two nations will be rivals. One nation will be stronger than the other; and your older son will serve your younger son.”
1_25_23.WEB: Yahweh said to her, “Two nations are in your womb. Two peoples will be separated from your body. The one people will be stronger than the other people. The elder will serve the younger.”
1_25_23.CSB17: And the LORD said to her: Two nations are in your womb; two peoples will come from you and be separated. One people will be stronger than the other, and the older will serve the younger.
1_25_23.AMP: The LORD said to her, "[The founders of] two nations are in your womb; And the separation of two nations has begun in your body; The one people shall be stronger than the other; And the older shall serve the younger."
1_25_23.MSG: God told her,Two nations are in your womb,two peoples butting heads while still in your body.One people will overpower the other,and the older will serve the younger.
1_25_23.NIV: The Lord said to her,“Two nations are in your womb,and two peoples from within you will be separated;one people will be stronger than the other,and the older will serve the younger.”
1_25_23.NRSVCE: And the LORD said to her, “Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples born of you shall be divided; the one shall be stronger than the other, the elder shall serve the younger.”

1_25_25.NKJV: And the first came out red. He was like a hairy garment all over; so they called his name Esau.

1_25_30.ESV: And Esau said to Jacob, “Let me eat some of that red stew, for I am exhausted!” (Therefore his name was called Edom. )

1_26_7.NKJV: And the men of the place asked about his wife. And he said, “She is my sister”; for he was afraid to say, “She is my wife,” because he thought, “lest the men of the place kill me for Rebekah, because she is beautiful to behold.”

1_26_7.MSG: The men of the place questioned him about his wife. He said, "She's my sister." He was afraid to say "She's my wife." He was thinking, "These men might kill me to get Rebekah, she's so beautiful."

1_26_9.NKJV: Then Abimelech called Isaac and said, “Quite obviously she is your wife; so how could you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac said to him, “Because I said, ‘Lest I die on account of her.’ ”

1_26_10.NKJV: And Abimelech said, “What is this you have done to us? One of the people might soon have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt on us.”

1_27_46.NKJV: And Rebekah said to Isaac, “I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth; if Jacob takes a wife of the daughters of Heth, like these who are the daughters of the land, what good will my life be to me?”
1_27_46.KJV: And Rebekah said to Isaac, I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth: if Jacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these which are of the daughters of the land, what good shall my life do me?

1_27_46.AMP: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, "I am tired of living because of the daughters of Heth [these insolent wives of Esau]. If Jacob takes a wife from the daughters of Heth, like these daughters of the land, what good will my life be to me?"
1_27_46.MSG: Rebekah spoke to Isaac, "I'm sick to death of these Hittite women. If Jacob also marries a native Hittite woman, why live?"

1_29_23.NKJV: Now it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter and brought her to Jacob; and he went in to her.
1_29_23.KJV: And it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter, and brought her to him; and he went in unto her.
1_29_23.ESV: But in the evening he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob, and he went in to her.
1_29_23.NASB: Now in the evening he took his daughter Leah, and brought her to him; and [Jacob] went in to her.
1_29_23.NLT: But that night, when it was dark, Laban took Leah to Jacob, and he slept with her.
1_29_23.WEB: In the evening, he took Leah his daughter, and brought her to him. He went in to her.
1_29_23.CSB17: That evening, Laban took his daughter Leah and gave her to Jacob, and he slept with her.
1_29_23.AMP: But in the evening he took Leah his daughter and brought her to Jacob, and Jacob went in to [consummate the marriage with] her.
1_29_23.MSG: At evening, though, he got his daughter Leah and brought her to the marriage bed, and Jacob slept with her.
1_29_23.NIV: But when evening came, he took his daughter Leah and gave her to Jacob, and Jacob lay with her.
1_29_23.NRSVCE: But in the evening he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob; and he went in to her.

1_29_24.NKJV: And Laban gave his maid Zilpah to his daughter Leah as a maid.
1_29_24.KJV: And Laban gave unto his daughter Leah Zilpah his maid for an handmaid.

1_29_24.NLT: (Laban had given Leah a servant, Zilpah, to be her maid.)
1_29_24.WEB: Laban gave Zilpah his servant to his daughter Leah for a servant.
1_29_24.CSB17: And Laban gave his slave Zilpah to his daughter Leah as her slave.
1_29_24.AMP: Laban also gave Zilpah his maid to his daughter Leah as a maid.

1_29_24.NIV: And Laban gave his servant girl Zilpah to his daughter as her maidservant.
1_29_24.NRSVCE: (Laban gave his maid Zilpah to his daughter Leah to be her maid.)

1_29_25.NKJV: So it came to pass in the morning, that behold, it was Leah. And he said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served you? Why then have you deceived me?”
1_29_25.KJV: And it came to pass, that in the morning, behold, it was Leah: and he said to Laban, What is this thou hast done unto me? did not I serve with thee for Rachel? wherefore then hast thou beguiled me?
1_29_25.ESV: And in the morning, behold, it was Leah! And Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?”
1_29_25.NASB: So it came about in the morning that, behold, it was Leah! And he said to Laban, 'What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served with you? Why then have you deceived me?'
1_29_25.NLT: But when Jacob woke up in the morning — it was Leah! “What have you done to me?” Jacob raged at Laban. “I worked seven years for Rachel! Why have you tricked me?”
1_29_25.WEB: In the morning, behold, it was Leah. He said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Didn’t I serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?”
1_29_25.CSB17: When morning came, there was Leah! So he said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Wasn’t it for Rachel that I worked for you? Why have you deceived me?”
1_29_25.AMP: But in the morning [when Jacob awoke], it was Leah [who was with him]! And he said to Laban, "What is this that you have done to me? Did I not work for you [for seven years] for Rachel? Why have you deceived and betrayed me [like this]?"
1_29_25.MSG: Morning came: There was Leah in the marriage bed!Jacob confronted Laban, "What have you done to me? Didn't I work all this time for the hand of Rachel? Why did you cheat me?"
1_29_25.NIV: When morning came, there was Leah! So Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? I served you for Rachel, didn’t I? Why have you deceived me?”
1_29_25.NRSVCE: When morning came, it was Leah! And Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?”

1_29_26.NKJV: And Laban said, “It must not be done so in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn.
1_29_26.KJV: And Laban said, It must not be so done in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn. country: Heb. place
1_29_26.ESV: Laban said, “It is not so done in our country, to give the younger before the firstborn.
1_29_26.NASB: But Laban said, 'It is not the practice in our place to marry off the younger before the firstborn.
1_29_26.NLT: “It’s not our custom here to marry off a younger daughter ahead of the firstborn,” Laban replied.
1_29_26.WEB: Laban said, “It is not done so in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn.
1_29_26.CSB17: Laban answered, “It is not the custom in this place to give the younger daughter in marriage before the firstborn.
1_29_26.AMP: But Laban only said, "It is not the tradition here to give the younger [daughter in marriage] before the older.
1_29_26.MSG: "We don't do it that way in our country," said Laban. "We don't marry off the younger daughter before the older.
1_29_26.NIV: Laban replied, “It is not our custom here to give the younger daughter in marriage before the older one.
1_29_26.NRSVCE: Laban said, “This is not done in our country — giving the younger before the firstborn.

1_29_27.NKJV: Fulfill her week, and we will give you this one also for the service which you will serve with me still another seven years.”
1_29_27.KJV: Fulfil her week, and we will give thee this also for the service which thou shalt serve with me yet seven other years.
1_29_27.ESV: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years.”
1_29_27.NASB: 'Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also for the service which you shall serve with me for another seven years.'
1_29_27.NLT: “But wait until the bridal week is over; then we’ll give you Rachel, too — provided you promise to work another seven years for me.”
1_29_27.WEB: Fulfill the week of this one, and we will give you the other also for the service which you will serve with me yet seven other years.”
1_29_27.CSB17: Complete this week of wedding celebration, and we will also give you this younger one in return for working yet another seven years for me.”
1_29_27.AMP: Finish the week [of the wedding feast] for Leah; then we will give you Rachel also, and in return you shall work for me for seven more years."
1_29_27.MSG: Enjoy your week of honeymoon, and then we'll give you the other one also. But it will cost you another seven years of work."
1_29_27.NIV: Finish this daughter’s bridal week; then we will give you the younger one also, in return for another seven years of work.”
1_29_27.NRSVCE: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years.”

1_29_28.NKJV: Then Jacob did so and fulfilled her week. So he gave him his daughter Rachel as wife also.
1_29_28.KJV: And Jacob did so, and fulfilled her week: and he gave him Rachel his daughter to wife also.
1_29_28.ESV: Jacob did so, and completed her week. Then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel to be his wife.
1_29_28.NASB: Jacob did so and completed her week, and he gave him his daughter Rachel as his wife.
1_29_28.NLT: So Jacob agreed to work seven more years. A week after Jacob had married Leah, Laban gave him Rachel, too.
1_29_28.WEB: Jacob did so, and fulfilled her week. He gave him Rachel his daughter as wife.
1_29_28.CSB17: And Jacob did just that. He finished the week of celebration, and Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as his wife.
1_29_28.AMP: So Jacob complied and fulfilled Leah's week [of celebration]; then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as his [second] wife.
1_29_28.MSG: Jacob agreed. When he'd completed the honeymoon week, Laban gave him his daughter Rachel to be his wife.
1_29_28.NIV: And Jacob did so. He finished the week with Leah, and then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel to be his wife.
1_29_28.NRSVCE: Jacob did so, and completed her week; then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as a wife.

1_29_29.NKJV: And Laban gave his maid Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as a maid.

1_29_29.NASB: Laban also gave his maid Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her maid.
1_29_29.NLT: (Laban gave Rachel a servant, Bilhah, to be her maid.)
1_29_29.WEB: Laban gave to Rachel his daughter Bilhah, his servant, to be her servant.
1_29_29.CSB17: And Laban gave his slave Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her slave.
1_29_29.AMP: Laban also gave Bilhah his maid to his daughter Rachel as a maid.
1_29_29.MSG: (Laban gave his maid Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her maid.)
1_29_29.NIV: Laban gave his servant girl Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her maidservant.
1_29_29.NRSVCE: (Laban gave his maid Bilhah to his daughter Rachel to be her maid.)

1_29_30.NKJV: Then Jacob also went in to Rachel, and he also loved Rachel more than Leah. And he served with Laban still another seven years.
1_29_30.KJV: And he went in also unto Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah, and served with him yet seven other years.
1_29_30.ESV: So Jacob went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and served Laban for another seven years.
1_29_30.NASB: So [Jacob] went in to Rachel also, and indeed he loved Rachel more than Leah, and he served with Laban for another seven years.
1_29_30.NLT: So Jacob slept with Rachel, too, and he loved her much more than Leah. He then stayed and worked for Laban the additional seven years.
1_29_30.WEB: He went in also to Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah, and served with him yet seven other years.
1_29_30.CSB17: Jacob slept with Rachel also, and indeed, he loved Rachel more than Leah. And he worked for Laban another seven years.
1_29_30.AMP: So Jacob consummated his marriage and lived with Rachel [as his wife], and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and he served with Laban for another seven years.
1_29_30.MSG: Jacob then slept with her. And he loved Rachel more than Leah. He worked for Laban another seven years.
1_29_30.NIV: Jacob lay with Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah. And he worked for Laban another seven years.
1_29_30.NRSVCE: So Jacob went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah. He served Laban for another seven years.

1_29_31.NKJV: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, He opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.KJV: And when the LORD saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb: but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.ESV: When the LORD saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.NASB: Now the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, and He opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.NLT: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, he enabled her to have children, but Rachel could not conceive.
1_29_31.WEB: Yahweh saw that Leah was hated, and he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.CSB17: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb; but Rachel was unable to conceive.
1_29_31.AMP: Now when the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, He made her able to bear children, but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.MSG: When God realized that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb. But Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.NIV: Jacob’s ChildrenWhen the Lord saw that Leah was not loved, he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.
1_29_31.NRSVCE: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb; but Rachel was barren.

1_29_32.NKJV: So Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben; for she said, “The LORD has surely looked on my affliction. Now therefore, my husband will love me.”
1_29_32.KJV: And Leah conceived, and bare a son, and she called his name Reuben: for she said, Surely the LORD hath looked upon my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me. Reuben: that is, See a son
1_29_32.ESV: And Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben, for she said, “Because the LORD has looked upon my affliction; for now my husband will love me.”
1_29_32.NASB: Leah conceived and bore a son and named him Reuben, for she said, 'Because the LORD has seen my affliction; surely now my husband will love me.'
1_29_32.NLT: So Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Reuben, for she said, “The LORD has noticed my misery, and now my husband will love me.”
1_29_32.WEB: Leah conceived, and bore a son, and she named him Reuben. For she said, “Because Yahweh has looked at my affliction. For now my husband will love me.”
1_29_32.CSB17: Leah conceived, gave birth to a son, and named him Reuben, for she said, “The LORD has seen my affliction; surely my husband will love me now.”
1_29_32.AMP: Leah conceived and gave birth to a son and named him Reuben (See, a son!), for she said, "Because the LORD has seen my humiliation and suffering; now my husband will love me [since I have given him a son]."
1_29_32.MSG: Leah became pregnant and had a son. She named him Reuben (Look-It's-a-Boy!). "This is a sign," she said, "that God has seen my misery; and a sign that now my husband will love me."
1_29_32.NIV: Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Reuben, for she said, “It is because the Lord has seen my misery. Surely my husband will love me now.”
1_29_32.NRSVCE: Leah conceived and bore a son, and she named him Reuben; for she said, “Because the LORD has looked on my affliction; surely now my husband will love me.”

1_29_33.NKJV: Then she conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has therefore given me this son also.” And she called his name Simeon.
1_29_33.KJV: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Because the LORD hath heard that I was hated, he hath therefore given me this son also: and she called his name Simeon. Simeon: that is, Hearing
1_29_33.ESV: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Because the LORD has heard that I am hated, he has given me this son also.” And she called his name Simeon.
1_29_33.NASB: Then she conceived again and bore a son and said, 'Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has therefore given me this [son] also.' So she named him Simeon.
1_29_33.NLT: She soon became pregnant again and gave birth to another son. She named him Simeon, for she said, “The LORD heard that I was unloved and has given me another son.”
1_29_33.WEB: She conceived again, and bore a son, and said, “Because Yahweh has heard that I am hated, he has therefore given me this son also.” She named him Simeon.
1_29_33.CSB17: She conceived again, gave birth to a son, and said, “The LORD heard that I am unloved and has given me this son also.” So she named him Simeon.
1_29_33.AMP: Then she conceived again and gave birth to a son and said, "Because the LORD heard that I am unloved, He has given me this son also." So she named him Simeon (God hears).
1_29_33.MSG: She became pregnant again and had another son. "God heard," she said, "that I was unloved and so he gave me this son also." She named this one Simeon (God-Heard).
1_29_33.NIV: She conceived again, and when she gave birth to a son she said, “Because the Lord heard that I am not loved, he gave me this one too.” So she named him Simeon.
1_29_33.NRSVCE: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Because the LORD has heard that I am hated, he has given me this son also”; and she named him Simeon.

1_29_34.NKJV: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Now this time my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.” Therefore his name was called Levi.
1_29_34.KJV: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Now this time will my husband be joined unto me, because I have born him three sons: therefore was his name called Levi. Levi: that is, Joined
1_29_34.ESV: Again she conceived and bore a son, and said, “Now this time my husband will be attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.” Therefore his name was called Levi.
1_29_34.NASB: She conceived again and bore a son and said, 'Now this time my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.' Therefore he was named Levi.
1_29_34.NLT: Then she became pregnant a third time and gave birth to another son. He was named Levi, for she said, “Surely this time my husband will feel affection for me, since I have given him three sons!”
1_29_34.WEB: She conceived again, and bore a son. Said, “Now this time will my husband be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons.” Therefore his name was called Levi.
1_29_34.CSB17: She conceived again, gave birth to a son, and said, “At last, my husband will become attached to me because I have borne three sons for him.” Therefore he was named Levi.
1_29_34.AMP: She conceived again and gave birth to a son and said, "Now this time my husband will become attached to me [as a companion], for I have given him three sons." Therefore he was named Levi.
1_29_34.MSG: She became pregnant yet again—another son. She said, "Now maybe my husband will connect with me—I've given him three sons!" That's why she named him Levi (Connect).
1_29_34.NIV: Again she conceived, and when she gave birth to a son she said, “Now at last my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.” So he was named Levi.
1_29_34.NRSVCE: Again she conceived and bore a son, and said, “Now this time my husband will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons”; therefore he was named Levi.

1_29_35.NKJV: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, “Now I will praise the LORD.” Therefore she called his name Judah. Then she stopped bearing.
1_29_35.KJV: And she conceived again, and bare a son: and she said, Now will I praise the LORD: therefore she called his name Judah; and left bearing. Judah: that is, Praise left: Heb. stood from bearing
1_29_35.ESV: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, “This time I will praise the LORD.” Therefore she called his name Judah. Then she ceased bearing.
1_29_35.NASB: And she conceived again and bore a son and said, 'This time I will praise the LORD.' Therefore she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing.
1_29_35.NLT: Once again Leah became pregnant and gave birth to another son. She named him Judah, for she said, “Now I will praise the LORD!” And then she stopped having children.
1_29_35.WEB: She conceived again, and bore a son. She said, “This time will I praise Yahweh.” Therefore she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing.
1_29_35.CSB17: And she conceived again, gave birth to a son, and said, “This time I will praise the LORD.” Therefore she named him Judah. Then Leah stopped having children.
1_29_35.AMP: Again she conceived and gave birth to a [fourth] son, and she said, "Now I will praise the LORD." So she named him Judah; then [for a time] she stopped bearing [children].
1_29_35.MSG: She became pregnant a final time and had a fourth son. She said, "This time I'll praise God." So she named him Judah (Praise-God). Then she stopped having children.
1_29_35.NIV: She conceived again, and when she gave birth to a son she said, “This time I will praise the Lord.” So she named him Judah. Then she stopped having children.
1_29_35.NRSVCE: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, “This time I will praise the LORD”; therefore she named him Judah; then she ceased bearing.

1_30_1.NKJV: Now when Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister, and said to Jacob, “Give me children, or else I die!”
1_30_1.KJV: And when Rachel saw that she bare Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister; and said unto Jacob, Give me children, or else I die.
1_30_1.ESV: When Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, she envied her sister. She said to Jacob, “Give me children, or I shall die!”
1_30_1.NASB: Now when Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, she became jealous of her sister; and she said to Jacob, 'Give me children, or else I die.'
1_30_1.NLT: When Rachel saw that she wasn’t having any children for Jacob, she became jealous of her sister. She pleaded with Jacob, “Give me children, or I’ll die!”
1_30_1.WEB: When Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister. She said to Jacob, “Give me children, or else I will die.”
1_30_1.CSB17: When Rachel saw that she was not bearing Jacob any children, she envied her sister. “Give me sons, or I will die!” she said to Jacob.
1_30_1.AMP: When Rachel saw that she conceived no children for Jacob, she envied her sister, and said to Jacob, "Give me children, or else I will die."
1_30_1.MSG: When Rachel realized that she wasn't having any children for Jacob, she became jealous of her sister. She told Jacob, "Give me sons or I'll die!"
1_30_1.NIV: When Rachel saw that she was not bearing Jacob any children, she became jealous of her sister. So she said to Jacob, “Give me children, or I’ll die!”
1_30_1.NRSVCE: When Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, she envied her sister; and she said to Jacob, “Give me children, or I shall die!”

1_30_2.NKJV: And Jacob’s anger was aroused against Rachel, and he said, “Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”
1_30_2.KJV: And Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel: and he said, Am I in God's stead, who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?
1_30_2.ESV: Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel, and he said, “Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”
1_30_2.NASB: Then Jacob's anger burned against Rachel, and he said, 'Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?'
1_30_2.NLT: Then Jacob became furious with Rachel. “Am I God?” he asked. “He’s the one who has kept you from having children!”
1_30_2.WEB: Jacob’s anger burned against Rachel, and he said, “Am I in God’s place, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”
1_30_2.CSB17: Jacob became angry with Rachel and said, “Am I in God’s place, who has withheld offspring from you?”
1_30_2.AMP: Then Jacob became furious with Rachel, and he said, "Am I in the place of God, who has denied you children?"
1_30_2.MSG: Jacob got angry with Rachel and said, "Am I God? Am I the one who refused you babies?"
1_30_2.NIV: Jacob became angry with her and said, “Am I in the place of God, who has kept you from having children?”
1_30_2.NRSVCE: Jacob became very angry with Rachel and said, “Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”

1_30_3.NKJV: So she said, “Here is my maid Bilhah; go in to her, and she will bear a child on my knees, that I also may have children by her.”
1_30_3.KJV: And she said, Behold my maid Bilhah, go in unto her; and she shall bear upon my knees, that I may also have children by her. have: Heb. be built by her
1_30_3.ESV: Then she said, “Here is my servant Bilhah; go in to her, so that she may give birth on my behalf, that even I may have children through her.”
1_30_3.NASB: She said, 'Here is my maid Bilhah, go in to her that she may bear on my knees, that through her I too may have children.'
1_30_3.NLT: Then Rachel told him, “Take my maid, Bilhah, and sleep with her. She will bear children for me, and through her I can have a family, too.”
1_30_3.WEB: She said, “Behold, my maid Bilhah. Go in to her, that she may bear on my knees, and I also may obtain children by her.”
1_30_3.CSB17: Then she said, “Here is my maid Bilhah. Go sleep with her, and she’ll bear children for me so that through her I too can build a family.”
1_30_3.AMP: She said, "Here, take my maid Bilhah and go in to her; and [when the baby comes] she shall deliver it [while sitting] on my knees, so that by her I may also have children [to count as my own]."
1_30_3.MSG: Rachel said, "Here's my maid Bilhah. Sleep with her. Let her substitute for me so I can have a child through her and build a family."
1_30_3.NIV: Then she said, “Here is Bilhah, my maidservant. Sleep with her so that she can bear children for me and that through her I too can build a family.”
1_30_3.NRSVCE: Then she said, “Here is my maid Bilhah; go in to her, that she may bear upon my knees and that I too may have children through her.”

1_30_4.NKJV: Then she gave him Bilhah her maid as wife, and Jacob went in to her.
1_30_4.KJV: And she gave him Bilhah her handmaid to wife: and Jacob went in unto her.

1_30_4.NASB: So she gave him her maid Bilhah as a wife, and Jacob went in to her.
1_30_4.NLT: So Rachel gave her servant, Bilhah, to Jacob as a wife, and he slept with her.

1_30_4.NRSVCE: So she gave him her maid Bilhah as a wife; and Jacob went in to her.

1_30_5.NKJV: And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_5.KJV: And Bilhah conceived, and bare Jacob a son.
1_30_5.ESV: And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_5.NASB: Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_5.NLT: Bilhah became pregnant and presented him with a son.
1_30_5.WEB: Bilhah conceived, and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_5.CSB17: Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_5.AMP: Bilhah conceived and gave birth to a son for Jacob.
1_30_5.MSG: Bilhah became pregnant and gave Jacob a son.
1_30_5.NIV: and she became pregnant and bore him a son.
1_30_5.NRSVCE: And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.

1_30_6.NKJV: Then Rachel said, “God has judged my case; and He has also heard my voice and given me a son.” Therefore she called his name Dan.
1_30_6.KJV: And Rachel said, God hath judged me, and hath also heard my voice, and hath given me a son: therefore called she his name Dan. Dan: that is, Judging

1_30_15.NKJV: But she said to her, “Is it a small matter that you have taken away my husband? Would you take away my son’s mandrakes also?” And Rachel said, “Therefore he will lie with you tonight for your son’s mandrakes.”
