1_31_16.AMP: Surely all the riches which God has taken from our father are ours and our children's. Now then, whatever God has told you to do, do it."
1_31_16.MSG: Any wealth that God has seen fit to return to us from our father is justly ours and our children's. Go ahead. Do what God told you."
1_31_16.NIV: Surely all the wealth that God took away from our father belongs to us and our children. So do whatever God has told you.”
1_31_16.NRSVCE: All the property that God has taken away from our father belongs to us and to our children; now then, do whatever God has said to you.”

1_31_17.NKJV: Then Jacob rose and set his sons and his wives on camels.
1_31_17.KJV: Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels;
1_31_17.ESV: So Jacob arose and set his sons and his wives on camels.
1_31_17.NASB: Then Jacob arose and put his children and his wives upon camels;
1_31_17.NLT: So Jacob put his wives and children on camels,
1_31_17.WEB: Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives on the camels,
1_31_17.CSB17: So Jacob got up and put his children and wives on the camels.
1_31_17.AMP: Then Jacob stood [and took action] and put his children and his wives on camels;
1_31_17.MSG: Jacob did it. He put his children and his wives on camels
1_31_17.NIV: Then Jacob put his children and his wives on camels,
1_31_17.NRSVCE: So Jacob arose, and set his children and his wives on camels;

1_31_18.NKJV: And he carried away all his livestock and all his possessions which he had gained, his acquired livestock which he had gained in Padan Aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
1_31_18.KJV: And he carried away all his cattle, and all his goods which he had gotten, the cattle of his getting, which he had gotten in Padanaram, for to go to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.
1_31_18.ESV: He drove away all his livestock, all his property that he had gained, the livestock in his possession that he had acquired in Paddan-aram, to go to the land of Canaan to his father Isaac.
1_31_18.NASB: and he drove away all his livestock and all his property which he had gathered, his acquired livestock which he had gathered in Paddan-aram, to go to the land of Canaan to his father Isaac.
1_31_18.NLT: and he drove all his livestock in front of him. He packed all the belongings he had acquired in Paddan-aram and set out for the land of Canaan, where his father, Isaac, lived.
1_31_18.WEB: and he took away all his livestock, and all his possessions which he had gathered, including the livestock which he had gained in Paddan Aram, to go to Isaac his father, to the land of Canaan.
1_31_18.CSB17: He took all the livestock and possessions he had acquired in Paddan-aram, and he drove his herds to go to the land of Canaan, to his father Isaac.
1_31_18.AMP: and he drove away all his livestock and [took along] all his property which he had acquired, the livestock he had obtained and accumulated in Paddan-aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
1_31_18.MSG: and gathered all his livestock and everything he had gotten, everything acquired in Paddan Aram, to go back home to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
1_31_18.NIV: and he drove all his livestock ahead of him, along with all the goods he had accumulated in Paddan Aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
1_31_18.NRSVCE: and he drove away all his livestock, all the property that he had gained, the livestock in his possession that he had acquired in Paddan-aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.

1_31_19.NKJV: Now Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel had stolen the household idols that were her father’s.
1_31_19.KJV: And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel had stolen the images that were her father's. images: Heb. teraphim
1_31_19.ESV: Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel stole her father's household gods.
1_31_19.NASB: When Laban had gone to shear his flock, then Rachel stole the household idols that were her father's.
1_31_19.NLT: At the time they left, Laban was some distance away, shearing his sheep. Rachel stole her father’s household idols and took them with her.
1_31_19.WEB: Now Laban had gone to shear his sheep: and Rachel stole the teraphim that were her father’s.
1_31_19.CSB17: When Laban had gone to shear his sheep, Rachel stole her father’s household idols.
1_31_19.AMP: When Laban had gone to shear his sheep, Rachel [went inside the house and] stole her father's household gods.
1_31_19.MSG: Laban was off shearing sheep. Rachel stole her father's household gods.
1_31_19.NIV: When Laban had gone to shear his sheep, Rachel stole her father’s household gods.
1_31_19.NRSVCE: Now Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel stole her father’s household gods.

1_31_20.NKJV: And Jacob stole away, unknown to Laban the Syrian, in that he did not tell him that he intended to flee.
1_31_20.KJV: And Jacob stole away unawares to Laban the Syrian, in that he told him not that he fled. unawares: Heb. the heart of Laban
1_31_20.ESV: And Jacob tricked Laban the Aramean, by not telling him that he intended to flee.
1_31_20.NASB: And Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean by not telling him that he was fleeing.
1_31_20.NLT: Jacob outwitted Laban the Aramean, for they set out secretly and never told Laban they were leaving.
1_31_20.WEB: Jacob deceived Laban the Syrian, in that he didn’t tell him that he was running away.
1_31_20.CSB17: And Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean, not telling him that he was fleeing.
1_31_20.AMP: And Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean (Syrian) by not telling him that he intended to leave and he slipped away secretly.
1_31_20.MSG: And Jacob had concealed his plans so well that Laban the Aramean had no idea what was going on—he was totally in the dark.
1_31_20.NIV: Moreover, Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean by not telling him he was running away.
1_31_20.NRSVCE: And Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean, in that he did not tell him that he intended to flee.

1_31_21.NKJV: So he fled with all that he had. He arose and crossed the river, and headed toward the mountains of Gilead.
1_31_21.KJV: So he fled with all that he had; and he rose up, and passed over the river, and set his face toward the mount Gilead.
1_31_21.ESV: He fled with all that he had and arose and crossed the Euphrates, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_21.NASB: So he fled with all that he had; and he arose and crossed the [Euphrates] River, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_21.NLT: So Jacob took all his possessions with him and crossed the Euphrates River, heading for the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_21.WEB: So he fled with all that he had. He rose up, passed over the River, and set his face toward the mountain of Gilead.
1_31_21.CSB17: He fled with all his possessions, crossed the Euphrates, and headed for the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_21.AMP: So he fled with everything that he had, and got up and crossed the river [Euphrates], and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead [east of the Jordan River].
1_31_21.MSG: Jacob got away with everything he had and was soon across the Euphrates headed for the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_21.NIV: So he fled with all he had, and crossing the River, he headed for the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_21.NRSVCE: So he fled with all that he had; starting out he crossed the Euphrates, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.

1_31_22.NKJV: And Laban was told on the third day that Jacob had fled.
1_31_22.KJV: And it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob was fled.
1_31_22.ESV: When it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob had fled,
1_31_22.NASB: When it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob had fled,
1_31_22.NLT: Three days later, Laban was told that Jacob had fled.
1_31_22.WEB: Laban was told on the third day that Jacob had fled.
1_31_22.CSB17: On the third day Laban was told that Jacob had fled.
1_31_22.AMP: On the third day [after his departure] Laban was told that Jacob had fled.
1_31_22.MSG: Three days later, Laban got the news: "Jacob's run off."
1_31_22.NIV: Laban Pursues JacobOn the third day Laban was told that Jacob had fled.
1_31_22.NRSVCE: On the third day Laban was told that Jacob had fled.

1_31_23.NKJV: Then he took his brethren with him and pursued him for seven days’ journey, and he overtook him in the mountains of Gilead.
1_31_23.KJV: And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days' journey; and they overtook him in the mount Gilead.
1_31_23.ESV: he took his kinsmen with him and pursued him for seven days and followed close after him into the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.NASB: then he took his kinsmen with him and pursued him [a distance of] seven days' journey, and he overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.NLT: So he gathered a group of his relatives and set out in hot pursuit. He caught up with Jacob seven days later in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.WEB: He took his relatives with him, and pursued him seven days’ journey. He overtook him in the mountain of Gilead.
1_31_23.CSB17: So he took his relatives with him, pursued Jacob for seven days, and overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.AMP: So he took his relatives with him and pursued him for seven days, and they overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.MSG: Laban rounded up his relatives and chased after him. Seven days later they caught up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.NIV: Taking his relatives with him, he pursued Jacob for seven days and caught up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_23.NRSVCE: So he took his kinsfolk with him and pursued him for seven days until he caught up with him in the hill country of Gilead.

1_31_24.NKJV: But God had come to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night, and said to him, “Be careful that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad.”
1_31_24.KJV: And God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night, and said unto him, Take heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad. either: Heb. from good to bad
1_31_24.ESV: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night and said to him, “Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.”
1_31_24.NASB: God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream of the night and said to him, 'Be careful that you do not speak to Jacob either good or bad.'
1_31_24.NLT: But the previous night God had appeared to Laban the Aramean in a dream and told him, “I’m warning you — leave Jacob alone!”
1_31_24.WEB: God came to Laban, the Syrian, in a dream of the night, and said to him, “Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.”
1_31_24.CSB17: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream at night. “Watch yourself!” God warned him. “Don’t say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.”
1_31_24.AMP: God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream at night and said to him, "Be careful that you do not speak to Jacob, either good or bad."
1_31_24.MSG: That night God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream and said, "Be careful what you do to Jacob, whether good or bad."
1_31_24.NIV: Then God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream at night and said to him, “Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.”
1_31_24.NRSVCE: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night, and said to him, “Take heed that you say not a word to Jacob, either good or bad.”

1_31_25.NKJV: So Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountains, and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mountains of Gilead.
1_31_25.KJV: Then Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mount: and Laban with his brethren pitched in the mount of Gilead.
1_31_25.ESV: And Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsmen pitched tents in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_25.NASB: Laban caught up with Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsmen camped in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_25.NLT: Laban caught up with Jacob as he was camped in the hill country of Gilead, and he set up his camp not far from Jacob’s.
1_31_25.WEB: Laban caught up with Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountain, and Laban with his relatives encamped in the mountain of Gilead.
1_31_25.CSB17: When Laban overtook Jacob, Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban and his relatives also pitched their tents in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_25.AMP: Then Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent on the hill, and Laban with his relatives camped on the same hill of Gilead.
1_31_25.MSG: When Laban reached him, Jacob's tents were pitched in the Gilead mountains; Laban pitched his tents there too.
1_31_25.NIV: Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country of Gilead when Laban overtook him, and Laban and his relatives camped there too.
1_31_25.NRSVCE: Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsfolk camped in the hill country of Gilead.

1_31_26.NKJV: And Laban said to Jacob: “What have you done, that you have stolen away unknown to me, and carried away my daughters like captives taken with the sword?
1_31_26.KJV: And Laban said to Jacob, What hast thou done, that thou hast stolen away unawares to me, and carried away my daughters, as captives taken with the sword?
1_31_26.ESV: And Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done, that you have tricked me and driven away my daughters like captives of the sword?
1_31_26.NASB: Then Laban said to Jacob, 'What have you done by deceiving me and carrying away my daughters like captives of the sword?
1_31_26.NLT: “What do you mean by deceiving me like this?” Laban demanded. “How dare you drag my daughters away like prisoners of war?
1_31_26.WEB: Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done, that you have deceived me, and carried away my daughters like captives of the sword?
1_31_26.CSB17: Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done? You have deceived me and taken my daughters away like prisoners of war!
1_31_26.AMP: Then Laban said to Jacob, "What do you mean by deceiving me and leaving without my knowledge, and carrying off my daughters as if [they were] captives of the sword?
1_31_26.MSG: "What do you mean," said Laban, "by keeping me in the dark and sneaking off, hauling my daughters off like prisoners of war?
1_31_26.NIV: Then Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done? You’ve deceived me, and you’ve carried off my daughters like captives in war.
1_31_26.NRSVCE: Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done? You have deceived me, and carried away my daughters like captives of the sword.

1_31_27.NKJV: Why did you flee away secretly, and steal away from me, and not tell me; for I might have sent you away with joy and songs, with timbrel and harp?
1_31_27.KJV: Wherefore didst thou flee away secretly, and steal away from me; and didst not tell me, that I might have sent thee away with mirth, and with songs, with tabret, and with harp? steal: Heb. hast stolen me
1_31_27.ESV: Why did you flee secretly and trick me, and did not tell me, so that I might have sent you away with mirth and songs, with tambourine and lyre?
1_31_27.NASB: 'Why did you flee secretly and deceive me, and did not tell me so that I might have sent you away with joy and with songs, with timbrel and with lyre;
1_31_27.NLT: Why did you slip away secretly? Why did you deceive me? And why didn’t you say you wanted to leave? I would have given you a farewell feast, with singing and music, accompanied by tambourines and harps.
1_31_27.WEB: Why did you flee secretly, and deceive me, and didn’t tell me, that I might have sent you away with mirth and with songs, with tambourine and with harp;
1_31_27.CSB17: Why did you secretly flee from me, deceive me, and not tell me? I would have sent you away with joy and singing, with tambourines and lyres,
1_31_27.AMP: Why did you run away secretly and deceive me and not tell me, so that [otherwise] I might have sent you away with joy and with songs, with [music on the] tambourine and lyre?
1_31_27.MSG: Why did you run off like a thief in the night? Why didn't you tell me? Why, I would have sent you off with a great celebration—music, timbrels, flutes!
1_31_27.NIV: Why did you run off secretly and deceive me? Why didn’t you tell me, so I could send you away with joy and singing to the music of tambourines and harps?
1_31_27.NRSVCE: Why did you flee secretly and deceive me and not tell me? I would have sent you away with mirth and songs, with tambourine and lyre.

1_31_28.NKJV: And you did not allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters. Now you have done foolishly in so doing.
1_31_28.KJV: And hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters? thou hast now done foolishly in so doing.
1_31_28.ESV: And why did you not permit me to kiss my sons and my daughters farewell? Now you have done foolishly.
1_31_28.NASB: and did not allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters? Now you have done foolishly.
1_31_28.NLT: Why didn’t you let me kiss my daughters and grandchildren and tell them good-bye? You have acted very foolishly!
1_31_28.WEB: and didn’t allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters? Now have you done foolishly.
1_31_28.CSB17: but you didn’t even let me kiss my grandchildren and my daughters. You have acted foolishly.
1_31_28.AMP: And why did you not allow me to kiss my grandchildren and my daughters [goodbye]? Now you have done a foolish thing [in behaving like this].
1_31_28.MSG: But you wouldn't permit me so much as a kiss for my daughters and grandchildren. It was a stupid thing for you to do.
1_31_28.NIV: You didn’t even let me kiss my grandchildren and my daughters good-by. You have done a foolish thing.
1_31_28.NRSVCE: And why did you not permit me to kiss my sons and my daughters farewell? What you have done is foolish.

1_31_29.NKJV: It is in my power to do you harm, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad.’
1_31_29.KJV: It is in the power of my hand to do you hurt: but the God of your father spake unto me yesternight, saying, Take thou heed that thou speak not to Jacob either good or bad.
1_31_29.ESV: It is in my power to do you harm. But the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.’
1_31_29.NASB: 'It is in my power to do you harm, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, 'Be careful not to speak either good or bad to Jacob.'
1_31_29.NLT: I could destroy you, but the God of your father appeared to me last night and warned me, ‘Leave Jacob alone!’
1_31_29.WEB: It is in the power of my hand to hurt you, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.’
1_31_29.CSB17: I could do you great harm, but last night the God of your father said to me: ‘Watch yourself! Don’t say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.’
1_31_29.AMP: It is in my power to harm you, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, 'Be careful not to speak to Jacob, either good or bad.'
1_31_29.MSG: If I had a mind to, I could destroy you right now, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, 'Be careful what you do to Jacob, whether good or bad.'
1_31_29.NIV: I have the power to harm you; but last night the God of your father said to me, ‘Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.’
1_31_29.NRSVCE: It is in my power to do you harm; but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Take heed that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad.’

1_31_30.NKJV: And now you have surely gone because you greatly long for your father’s house, but why did you steal my gods?”
1_31_30.KJV: And now, though thou wouldest needs be gone, because thou sore longedst after thy father's house, yet wherefore hast thou stolen my gods?
1_31_30.ESV: And now you have gone away because you longed greatly for your father's house, but why did you steal my gods?”
1_31_30.NASB: 'Now you have indeed gone away because you longed greatly for your father's house; [but] why did you steal my gods?'
1_31_30.NLT: I can understand your feeling that you must go, and your intense longing for your father’s home. But why have you stolen my gods?”
1_31_30.WEB: Now, you want to be gone, because you greatly longed for your father’s house, but why have you stolen my gods?”
1_31_30.CSB17: Now you have gone off because you long for your father’s family — but why have you stolen my gods?”
1_31_30.AMP: Now [I suppose] you felt you must go because you were homesick for your father's house and family; but why did you steal my [household] gods?"
1_31_30.MSG: I understand. You left because you were homesick. But why did you steal my household gods?"
1_31_30.NIV: Now you have gone off because you longed to return to your father’s house. But why did you steal my gods?”
1_31_30.NRSVCE: Even though you had to go because you longed greatly for your father’s house, why did you steal my gods?”

1_31_31.NKJV: Then Jacob answered and said to Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I said, ‘Perhaps you would take your daughters from me by force.’
1_31_31.KJV: And Jacob answered and said to Laban, Because I was afraid: for I said, Peradventure thou wouldest take by force thy daughters from me.
1_31_31.ESV: Jacob answered and said to Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I thought that you would take your daughters from me by force.
1_31_31.NASB: Then Jacob replied to Laban, 'Because I was afraid, for I thought that you would take your daughters from me by force.
1_31_31.NLT: “I rushed away because I was afraid,” Jacob answered. “I thought you would take your daughters from me by force.
1_31_31.WEB: Jacob answered Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I said, ‘Lest you should take your daughters from me by force.’
1_31_31.CSB17: Jacob answered, “I was afraid, for I thought you would take your daughters from me by force.
1_31_31.AMP: Jacob answered Laban, "[I left secretly] because I was afraid, for I thought you would take your daughters away from me by force.
1_31_31.MSG: Jacob answered Laban, "I was afraid. I thought you would take your daughters away from me by brute force.
1_31_31.NIV: Jacob answered Laban, “I was afraid, because I thought you would take your daughters away from me by force.
1_31_31.NRSVCE: Jacob answered Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I thought that you would take your daughters from me by force.

1_31_32.NKJV: With whomever you find your gods, do not let him live. In the presence of our brethren, identify what I have of yours and take it with you.” For Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_32.KJV: With whomsoever thou findest thy gods, let him not live: before our brethren discern thou what is thine with me, and take it to thee. For Jacob knew not that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_32.ESV: Anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live. In the presence of our kinsmen point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_32.NASB: 'The one with whom you find your gods shall not live; in the presence of our kinsmen point out what is yours among my belongings and take [it] for yourself.' For Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_32.NLT: But as for your gods, see if you can find them, and let the person who has taken them die! And if you find anything else that belongs to you, identify it before all these relatives of ours, and I will give it back!” But Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
1_31_32.WEB: Anyone you find your gods with shall not live. Before our relatives, discern what is yours with me, and take it.” For Jacob didn’t know that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_32.CSB17: If you find your gods with anyone here, he will not live! Before our relatives, point out anything that is yours and take it.” Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen the idols.
1_31_32.AMP: The one with whom you find your gods shall not live; in the presence of our relatives [search my possessions and] point out whatever you find that belongs to you and take it." For Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen the idols.
1_31_32.MSG: But as far as your gods are concerned, if you find that anybody here has them, that person dies. With all of us watching, look around. If you find anything here that belongs to you, take it." Jacob didn't know that Rachel had stolen the gods.
1_31_32.NIV: But if you find anyone who has your gods, he shall not live. In the presence of our relatives, see for yourself whether there is anything of yours here with me; and if so, take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen the gods.
1_31_32.NRSVCE: But anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live. In the presence of our kinsfolk, point out what I have that is yours, and take it.” Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen the gods.

1_31_33.NKJV: And Laban went into Jacob’s tent, into Leah’s tent, and into the two maids’ tents, but he did not find them. Then he went out of Leah’s tent and entered Rachel’s tent.
1_31_33.KJV: And Laban went into Jacob's tent, and into Leah's tent, and into the two maidservants' tents; but he found them not. Then went he out of Leah's tent, and entered into Rachel's tent.
1_31_33.ESV: So Laban went into Jacob's tent and into Leah's tent and into the tent of the two female servants, but he did not find them. And he went out of Leah's tent and entered Rachel's.
1_31_33.NASB: So Laban went into Jacob's tent and into Leah's tent and into the tent of the two maids, but he did not find [them]. Then he went out of Leah's tent and entered Rachel's tent.
1_31_33.NLT: Laban went first into Jacob’s tent to search there, then into Leah’s, and then the tents of the two servant wives — but he found nothing. Finally, he went into Rachel’s tent.
1_31_33.WEB: Laban went into Jacob’s tent, into Leah’s tent, and into the tent of the two female servants; but he didn’t find them. He went out of Leah’s tent, and entered into Rachel’s tent.
1_31_33.CSB17: So Laban went into Jacob’s tent, Leah’s tent, and the tents of the two concubines, but he found nothing. When he left Leah’s tent, he went into Rachel’s tent.
1_31_33.AMP: So Laban went into Jacob's tent and into Leah's tent and the tent of the two maids, but he did not find them. Then he came out of Leah's tent and entered Rachel's tent.
1_31_33.MSG: Laban went through Jacob's tent, Leah's tent, and the tents of the two maids but didn't find them. He went from Leah's tent to Rachel's.
1_31_33.NIV: So Laban went into Jacob’s tent and into Leah’s tent and into the tent of the two maidservants, but he found nothing. After he came out of Leah’s tent, he entered Rachel’s tent.
1_31_33.NRSVCE: So Laban went into Jacob’s tent, and into Leah’s tent, and into the tent of the two maids, but he did not find them. And he went out of Leah’s tent, and entered Rachel’s.

1_31_34.NKJV: Now Rachel had taken the household idols, put them in the camel’s saddle, and sat on them. And Laban searched all about the tent but did not find them.
1_31_34.KJV: Now Rachel had taken the images, and put them in the camel's furniture, and sat upon them. And Laban searched all the tent, but found them not. searched: Heb. felt
1_31_34.ESV: Now Rachel had taken the household gods and put them in the camel's saddle and sat on them. Laban felt all about the tent, but did not find them.
1_31_34.NASB: Now Rachel had taken the household idols and put them in the camel's saddle, and she sat on them. And Laban felt through all the tent but did not find [them].
1_31_34.NLT: But Rachel had taken the household idols and hidden them in her camel saddle, and now she was sitting on them. When Laban had thoroughly searched her tent without finding them,
1_31_34.WEB: Now Rachel had taken the teraphim, put them in the camel’s saddle, and sat on them. Laban felt around all the tent, but didn’t find them.
1_31_34.CSB17: Now Rachel had taken Laban’s household idols, put them in the saddlebag of the camel, and sat on them. Laban searched the whole tent but found nothing.
1_31_34.AMP: Now Rachel had taken the household idols and put them in the camel's saddlebag and sat on them. Laban searched through all her tent, but did not find them.
1_31_34.MSG: But Rachel had taken the household gods, put them inside a camel cushion, and was sitting on them. When Laban had gone through the tent, searching high and low without finding a thing,
1_31_34.NIV: Now Rachel had taken the household gods and put them inside her camel’s saddle and was sitting on them. Laban searched through everything in the tent but found nothing.
1_31_34.NRSVCE: Now Rachel had taken the household gods and put them in the camel’s saddle, and sat on them. Laban felt all about in the tent, but did not find them.

1_31_35.NKJV: And she said to her father, “Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise before you, for the manner of women is with me.” And he searched but did not find the household idols.
1_31_35.KJV: And she said to her father, Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise up before thee; for the custom of women is upon me. And he searched, but found not the images.
1_31_35.ESV: And she said to her father, “Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me.” So he searched but did not find the household gods.
1_31_35.NASB: She said to her father, 'Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the manner of women is upon me.' So he searched but did not find the household idols.
1_31_35.NLT: she said to her father, “Please, sir, forgive me if I don’t get up for you. I’m having my monthly period.” So Laban continued his search, but he could not find the household idols.
1_31_35.WEB: She said to her father, “Don’t let my lord be angry that I can’t rise up before you; for I’m having my period.” He searched, but didn’t find the teraphim.
1_31_35.CSB17: She said to her father, “Don’t be angry, my lord, that I cannot stand up in your presence; I am having my period.” So Laban searched, but could not find the household idols.
1_31_35.AMP: So Rachel said to her father, "Do not be displeased, my lord, that I cannot rise before you, for the manner of women is on me and I am unwell." He searched [further] but did not find the household idols.
1_31_35.MSG: Rachel said to her father, "Don't think I'm being disrespectful, my master, that I can't stand before you, but I'm having my period." So even though he turned the place upside down in his search, he didn't find the household gods.
1_31_35.NIV: Rachel said to her father, “Don’t be angry, my lord, that I cannot stand up in your presence; I’m having my period.” So he searched but could not find the household gods.
1_31_35.NRSVCE: And she said to her father, “Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me.” So he searched, but did not find the household gods.

1_31_36.NKJV: Then Jacob was angry and rebuked Laban, and Jacob answered and said to Laban: “What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have so hotly pursued me?
1_31_36.KJV: And Jacob was wroth, and chode with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my trespass? what is my sin, that thou hast so hotly pursued after me?
1_31_36.ESV: Then Jacob became angry and berated Laban. Jacob said to Laban, “What is my offense? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?
1_31_36.NASB: Then Jacob became angry and contended with Laban; and Jacob said to Laban, 'What is my transgression? What is my sin that you have hotly pursued me?
1_31_36.NLT: Then Jacob became very angry, and he challenged Laban. “What’s my crime?” he demanded. “What have I done wrong to make you chase after me as though I were a criminal?
1_31_36.WEB: Jacob was angry, and argued with Laban. Jacob answered Laban, “What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?
1_31_36.CSB17: Then Jacob became incensed and brought charges against Laban. “What is my crime?” he said to Laban. “What is my sin, that you have pursued me?
1_31_36.AMP: Then Jacob became angry and argued with Laban. And he said to Laban, "What is my fault? What is my sin that you pursued me like this?
1_31_36.MSG: Now it was Jacob's turn to get angry. He lit into Laban: "So what's my crime, what wrong have I done you that you badger me like this?
1_31_36.NIV: Jacob was angry and took Laban to task. “What is my crime?” he asked Laban. “What sin have I committed that you hunt me down?
1_31_36.NRSVCE: Then Jacob became angry, and upbraided Laban. Jacob said to Laban, “What is my offense? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?

1_31_37.NKJV: Although you have searched all my things, what part of your household things have you found? Set it here before my brethren and your brethren, that they may judge between us both!
1_31_37.KJV: Whereas thou hast searched all my stuff, what hast thou found of all thy household stuff? set it here before my brethren and thy brethren, that they may judge betwixt us both. searched: Heb. felt
1_31_37.ESV: For you have felt through all my goods; what have you found of all your household goods? Set it here before my kinsmen and your kinsmen, that they may decide between us two.
1_31_37.NASB: 'Though you have felt through all my goods, what have you found of all your household goods? Set [it] here before my kinsmen and your kinsmen, that they may decide between us two.
1_31_37.NLT: You have rummaged through everything I own. Now show me what you found that belongs to you! Set it out here in front of us, before our relatives, for all to see. Let them judge between us!
1_31_37.WEB: Now that you have felt around in all my stuff, what have you found of all your household stuff? Set it here before my relatives and your relatives, that they may judge between us two.
1_31_37.CSB17: You’ve searched all my possessions! Have you found anything of yours? Put it here before my relatives and yours, and let them decide between the two of us.
1_31_37.AMP: Although you have searched through all my possessions, what have you found of your household goods? Put it here before my relatives and your relatives, so that they may decide [who has done right] between the two of us.
1_31_37.MSG: You've ransacked the place. Have you turned up a single thing that's yours? Let's see it—display the evidence. Our two families can be the jury and decide between us.
1_31_37.NIV: Now that you have searched through all my goods, what have you found that belongs to your household? Put it here in front of your relatives and mine, and let them judge between the two of us.
1_31_37.NRSVCE: Although you have felt about through all my goods, what have you found of all your household goods? Set it here before my kinsfolk and your kinsfolk, so that they may decide between us two.

1_31_38.NKJV: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried their young, and I have not eaten the rams of your flock.
1_31_38.KJV: This twenty years have I been with thee; thy ewes and thy she goats have not cast their young, and the rams of thy flock have I not eaten.
1_31_38.ESV: These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams of your flocks.
1_31_38.NASB: 'These twenty years I [have been] with you; your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried, nor have I eaten the rams of your flocks.
1_31_38.NLT: “For twenty years I have been with you, caring for your flocks. In all that time your sheep and goats never miscarried. In all those years I never used a single ram of yours for food.
1_31_38.WEB: “These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not cast their young, and I haven’t eaten the rams of your flocks.
1_31_38.CSB17: I’ve been with you these twenty years. Your ewes and female goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams from your flock.
1_31_38.AMP: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and your female goats have not lost their young, nor have I eaten the rams of your flocks.
1_31_38.MSG: "In the twenty years I've worked for you, ewes and she-goats never miscarried. I never feasted on the rams from your flock.
1_31_38.NIV: “I have been with you for twenty years now. Your sheep and goats have not miscarried, nor have I eaten rams from your flocks.
1_31_38.NRSVCE: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams of your flocks.

1_31_39.NKJV: That which was torn by beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it. You required it from my hand, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
1_31_39.KJV: That which was torn of beasts I brought not unto thee; I bare the loss of it; of my hand didst thou require it, whether stolen by day, or stolen by night.
1_31_39.ESV: What was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you. I bore the loss of it myself. From my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
1_31_39.NASB: 'That which was torn [of beasts] I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it myself. You required it of my hand [whether] stolen by day or stolen by night.
1_31_39.NLT: If any were attacked and killed by wild animals, I never showed you the carcass and asked you to reduce the count of your flock. No, I took the loss myself! You made me pay for every stolen animal, whether it was taken in broad daylight or in the dark of night.
1_31_39.WEB: That which was torn of animals, I didn’t bring to you. I bore its loss. Of my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
1_31_39.CSB17: I did not bring you any of the flock torn by wild beasts; I myself bore the loss. You demanded payment from me for what was stolen by day or by night.
1_31_39.AMP: I did not bring you the torn carcasses [of the animals attacked by predators]; I [personally] took the loss. You required of me [to make good] everything that was stolen, whether it occurred by day or night.
1_31_39.MSG: I never brought you a torn carcass killed by wild animals but that I paid for it out of my own pocket—actually, you made me pay whether it was my fault or not.
1_31_39.NIV: I did not bring you animals torn by wild beasts; I bore the loss myself. And you demanded payment from me for whatever was stolen by day or night.
1_31_39.NRSVCE: That which was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it myself; of my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.

1_31_40.NKJV: There I was! In the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night, and my sleep departed from my eyes.
1_31_40.KJV: Thus I was; in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep departed from mine eyes.
1_31_40.ESV: There I was: by day the heat consumed me, and the cold by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.
1_31_40.NASB: '[Thus] I was: by day the heat consumed me and the frost by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.
1_31_40.NLT: “I worked for you through the scorching heat of the day and through cold and sleepless nights.
1_31_40.WEB: This was my situation: in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep fled from my eyes.
1_31_40.CSB17: There I was — the heat consumed me by day and the frost by night, and sleep fled from my eyes.
1_31_40.AMP: This was my situation: by day the heat consumed me and by night the cold, and I could not sleep.
1_31_40.MSG: I was out in all kinds of weather, from torrid heat to freezing cold, putting in many a sleepless night.
1_31_40.NIV: This was my situation: The heat consumed me in the daytime and the cold at night, and sleep fled from my eyes.
1_31_40.NRSVCE: It was like this with me: by day the heat consumed me, and the cold by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.

1_31_41.NKJV: Thus I have been in your house twenty years; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.KJV: Thus have I been twenty years in thy house; I served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters, and six years for thy cattle: and thou hast changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.ESV: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.NASB: 'These twenty years I have been in your house; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.NLT: Yes, for twenty years I slaved in your house! I worked for fourteen years earning your two daughters, and then six more years for your flock. And you changed my wages ten times!
1_31_41.WEB: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.CSB17: For twenty years in your household I served you — fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flocks — and you have changed my wages ten times!
1_31_41.AMP: These twenty years I have been in your house; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for [my share of] your flocks, and you have changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.MSG: For twenty years I've done this: I slaved away fourteen years for your two daughters and another six years for your flock and you changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.NIV: It was like this for the twenty years I was in your household. I worked for you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flocks, and you changed my wages ten times.
1_31_41.NRSVCE: These twenty years I have been in your house; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.

1_31_42.NKJV: Unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.”
1_31_42.KJV: Except the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely thou hadst sent me away now empty. God hath seen mine affliction and the labour of my hands, and rebuked thee yesternight.
1_31_42.ESV: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God saw my affliction and the labor of my hands and rebuked you last night.”
1_31_42.NASB: 'If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had not been for me, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my affliction and the toil of my hands, so He rendered judgment last night.'
1_31_42.NLT: In fact, if the God of my father had not been on my side — the God of Abraham and the fearsome God of Isaac — you would have sent me away empty-handed. But God has seen your abuse and my hard work. That is why he appeared to you last night and rebuked you!”
1_31_42.WEB: Unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty. God has seen my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.”
1_31_42.CSB17: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, certainly now you would have sent me off empty-handed. But God has seen my affliction and my hard work, and he issued his verdict last night.”
1_31_42.AMP: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and [the Feared One] of Isaac, had not been with me, most certainly you would have sent me away now empty-handed. God has seen my affliction and humiliation and the [exhausting] labor of my hands, so He rendered judgment and rebuked you last night."
1_31_42.MSG: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not stuck with me, you would have sent me off penniless. But God saw the fix I was in and how hard I had worked and last night rendered his verdict."
1_31_42.NIV: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, you would surely have sent me away empty-handed. But God has seen my hardship and the toil of my hands, and last night he rebuked you.”
1_31_42.NRSVCE: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God saw my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.”

1_31_43.NKJV: And Laban answered and said to Jacob, “These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and this flock is my flock; all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day to these my daughters or to their children whom they have borne?
1_31_43.KJV: And Laban answered and said unto Jacob, These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and these cattle are my cattle, and all that thou seest is mine: and what can I do this day unto these my daughters, or unto their children which they have born?
1_31_43.ESV: Then Laban answered and said to Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day for these my daughters or for their children whom they have borne?
1_31_43.NASB: Then Laban replied to Jacob, 'The daughters are my daughters, and the children are my children, and the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day to these my daughters or to their children whom they have borne?
1_31_43.NLT: Then Laban replied to Jacob, “These women are my daughters, these children are my grandchildren, and these flocks are my flocks — in fact, everything you see is mine. But what can I do now about my daughters and their children?
1_31_43.WEB: Laban answered Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine: and what can I do today to these my daughters, or to their children whom they have borne?
1_31_43.CSB17: Then Laban answered Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters; the sons, my sons; and the flocks, my flocks! Everything you see is mine! But what can I do today for these daughters of mine or for the children they have borne?
1_31_43.AMP: Laban answered Jacob, "These women [that you married] are my daughters, these children are my grandchildren, these flocks are [from] my flocks, and all that you see [here] is mine. But what can I do today to these my daughters or to their children to whom they have given birth?
1_31_43.MSG: Laban defended himself: "The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flock is my flock—everything you see is mine. But what can I do about my daughters or for the children they've had?
1_31_43.NIV: Laban answered Jacob, “The women are my daughters, the children are my children, and the flocks are my flocks. All you see is mine. Yet what can I do today about these daughters of mine, or about the children they have borne?
1_31_43.NRSVCE: Then Laban answered and said to Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do today about these daughters of mine, or about their children whom they have borne?

1_31_44.NKJV: Now therefore, come, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it be a witness between you and me.”
1_31_44.KJV: Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be for a witness between me and thee.
1_31_44.ESV: Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I. And let it be a witness between you and me.”
1_31_44.NASB: 'So now come, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it be a witness between you and me.'
1_31_44.NLT: So come, let’s make a covenant, you and I, and it will be a witness to our commitment.”
1_31_44.WEB: Now come, let us make a covenant, you and I; and let it be for a witness between me and you.”
1_31_44.CSB17: Come now, let’s make a covenant, you and I. Let it be a witness between the two of us.”
1_31_44.AMP: So come now, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it serve as a witness between you and me."
1_31_44.MSG: So let's settle things between us, make a covenant—God will be the witness between us."
1_31_44.NIV: Come now, let’s make a covenant, you and I, and let it serve as a witness between us.”
1_31_44.NRSVCE: Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I; and let it be a witness between you and me.”

1_31_45.NKJV: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.
1_31_45.KJV: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.
1_31_45.ESV: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.
1_31_45.NASB: Then Jacob took a stone and set it up [as] a pillar.
1_31_45.NLT: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a monument.
1_31_45.WEB: Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.
1_31_45.CSB17: So Jacob picked out a stone and set it up as a marker.
1_31_45.AMP: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a [memorial] pillar.
1_31_45.MSG: Jacob took a stone and set it upright as a pillar.
1_31_45.NIV: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.
1_31_45.NRSVCE: So Jacob took a stone, and set it up as a pillar.

1_31_46.NKJV: Then Jacob said to his brethren, “Gather stones.” And they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there on the heap.
1_31_46.KJV: And Jacob said unto his brethren, Gather stones; and they took stones, and made an heap: and they did eat there upon the heap.
1_31_46.ESV: And Jacob said to his kinsmen, “Gather stones.” And they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there by the heap.
1_31_46.NASB: Jacob said to his kinsmen, 'Gather stones.' So they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there by the heap.
1_31_46.NLT: Then he told his family members, “Gather some stones.” So they gathered stones and piled them in a heap. Then Jacob and Laban sat down beside the pile of stones to eat a covenant meal.
1_31_46.WEB: Jacob said to his relatives, “Gather stones.” They took stones, and made a heap. They ate there by the heap.
1_31_46.CSB17: Then Jacob said to his relatives, “Gather stones.” And they took stones and made a mound, then ate there by the mound.
1_31_46.AMP: Jacob said to his relatives, "Gather stones." And they took stones and made a mound [of stones], and they ate [a ceremonial meal together] there on the mound [of stones].
1_31_46.MSG: Jacob called his family around, "Get stones!" They gathered stones and heaped them up and then ate there beside the pile of stones.
1_31_46.NIV: He said to his relatives, “Gather some stones.” So they took stones and piled them in a heap, and they ate there by the heap.
1_31_46.NRSVCE: And Jacob said to his kinsfolk, “Gather stones,” and they took stones, and made a heap; and they ate there by the heap.

1_31_47.NKJV: Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_47.KJV: And Laban called it Jegarsahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed. Jegarsahadutha: that is, The heap of witness, Chaldee Galeed: that is, The heap of witness, Heb.
1_31_47.ESV: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_47.NASB: Now Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_47.NLT: To commemorate the event, Laban called the place Jegar-sahadutha (which means “witness pile” in Aramaic), and Jacob called it Galeed (which means “witness pile” in Hebrew).
1_31_47.WEB: Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_47.CSB17: Laban named the mound Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob named it Galeed.
1_31_47.AMP: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha (stone monument of testimony in Aramaic), but Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_47.MSG: Laban named it in Aramaic, Yegar-sahadutha (Witness Monument); Jacob echoed the naming in Hebrew, Galeed (Witness Monument).
1_31_47.NIV: Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha, and Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_47.NRSVCE: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.

1_31_48.NKJV: And Laban said, “This heap is a witness between you and me this day.” Therefore its name was called Galeed,
1_31_48.KJV: And Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and thee this day. Therefore was the name of it called Galeed;
1_31_48.ESV: Laban said, “This heap is a witness between you and me today.” Therefore he named it Galeed,
1_31_48.NASB: Laban said, 'This heap is a witness between you and me this day.' Therefore it was named Galeed,
1_31_48.NLT: Then Laban declared, “This pile of stones will stand as a witness to remind us of the covenant we have made today.” This explains why it was called Galeed — “Witness Pile.”
1_31_48.WEB: Laban said, “This heap is witness between me and you today.” Therefore it was named Galeed
1_31_48.CSB17: Then Laban said, “This mound is a witness between you and me today.” Therefore the place was called Galeed
1_31_48.AMP: Laban said, "This mound [of stones] is a witness [a reminder of the oath taken] today between you and me." Therefore he [also] called the name Galeed,
1_31_48.MSG: Laban said, "This monument of stones will be a witness, beginning now, between you and me." (That's why it is called Galeed—Witness Monument.)
1_31_48.NIV: Laban said, “This heap is a witness between you and me today.” That is why it was called Galeed.
1_31_48.NRSVCE: Laban said, “This heap is a witness between you and me today.” Therefore he called it Galeed,

1_31_49.NKJV: also Mizpah, because he said, “May the LORD watch between you and me when we are absent one from another.
1_31_49.KJV: And Mizpah; for he said, The LORD watch between me and thee, when we are absent one from another. Mizpah: that is, A beacon, or, watchtower
1_31_49.ESV: and Mizpah, for he said, “The LORD watch between you and me, when we are out of one another's sight.
1_31_49.NASB: and Mizpah, for he said, 'May the LORD watch between you and me when we are absent one from the other.
1_31_49.NLT: But it was also called Mizpah (which means “watchtower”), for Laban said, “May the LORD keep watch between us to make sure that we keep this covenant when we are out of each other’s sight.
