1_41_42.WEB: Pharaoh took off his signet ring from his hand, and put it on Joseph’s hand, and arrayed him in robes of fine linen, and put a gold chain about his neck,
1_41_42.CSB17: Pharaoh removed his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph’s hand, clothed him with fine linen garments, and placed a gold chain around his neck.
1_41_42.AMP: Then Pharaoh took off his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's hand, and dressed him in [official] vestments of fine linen and put a gold chain around his neck.
1_41_42.MSG: Then Pharaoh removed his signet ring from his finger and slipped it on Joseph's hand. He outfitted him in robes of the best linen and put a gold chain around his neck.
1_41_42.NIV: Then Pharaoh took his signet ring from his finger and put it on Joseph’s finger. He dressed him in robes of fine linen and put a gold chain around his neck.
1_41_42.NRSVCE: Removing his signet ring from his hand, Pharaoh put it on Joseph’s hand; he arrayed him in garments of fine linen, and put a gold chain around his neck.

1_41_43.NKJV: And he had him ride in the second chariot which he had; and they cried out before him, “Bow the knee!” So he set him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_43.KJV: And he made him to ride in the second chariot which he had; and they cried before him, Bow the knee: and he made him ruler over all the land of Egypt. Bow: or, Tender father: Heb. Abrech
1_41_43.ESV: And he made him ride in his second chariot. And they called out before him, “Bow the knee!” Thus he set him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_43.NASB: He had him ride in his second chariot; and they proclaimed before him, 'Bow the knee!' And he set him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_43.NLT: Then he had Joseph ride in the chariot reserved for his second-in-command. And wherever Joseph went, the command was shouted, “Kneel down!” So Pharaoh put Joseph in charge of all Egypt.
1_41_43.WEB: and he made him to ride in the second chariot which he had. They cried before him, “Bow the knee!” He set him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_43.CSB17: He had Joseph ride in his second chariot, and servants called out before him, “Make way!” So he placed him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_43.AMP: He had him ride in his second chariot; and runners proclaimed before him, "[Attention,] bow the knee!" And he set him over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_43.MSG: He put the second-in-command chariot at his disposal, and as he rode people shouted "Bravo!"Joseph was in charge of the entire country of Egypt.
1_41_43.NIV: He had him ride in a chariot as his second-in-command, and men shouted before him, “Make way!” Thus he put him in charge of the whole land of Egypt.
1_41_43.NRSVCE: He had him ride in the chariot of his second-in-command; and they cried out in front of him, “Bow the knee!” Thus he set him over all the land of Egypt.

1_41_44.NKJV: Pharaoh also said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no man may lift his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.”
1_41_44.KJV: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph, I am Pharaoh, and without thee shall no man lift up his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.
1_41_44.ESV: Moreover, Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no one shall lift up hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.”
1_41_44.NASB: Moreover, Pharaoh said to Joseph, '[Though] I am Pharaoh, yet without your permission no one shall raise his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.'
1_41_44.NLT: And Pharaoh said to him, “I am Pharaoh, but no one will lift a hand or foot in the entire land of Egypt without your approval.”
1_41_44.WEB: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, and without you shall no man lift up his hand or his foot in all the land of Egypt.”
1_41_44.CSB17: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh and no one will be able to raise his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt without your permission.”
1_41_44.AMP: Moreover, Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Though I am Pharaoh, yet without your permission shall no man raise his hand [to do anything] or set his foot [to go anywhere] in all the land of Egypt [all classes of people shall submit to your authority]."
1_41_44.MSG: Pharaoh told Joseph, "I am Pharaoh, but no one in Egypt will make a single move without your stamp of approval."
1_41_44.NIV: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, but without your word no one will lift hand or foot in all Egypt.”
1_41_44.NRSVCE: Moreover Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no one shall lift up hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.”

1_41_45.NKJV: And Pharaoh called Joseph’s name Zaphnath-Paaneah. And he gave him as a wife Asenath, the daughter of Poti-Pherah priest of On. So Joseph went out over all the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.KJV: And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphnathpaaneah; and he gave him to wife Asenath the daughter of Potipherah priest of On. And Joseph went out over all the land of Egypt. Zaphnathpaaneah: which in the Coptic signifies, A revealer of secrets, or, The man to whom secrets are revealed priest: or, prince
1_41_45.ESV: And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphenath-paneah. And he gave him in marriage Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On. So Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.NASB: Then Pharaoh named Joseph Zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, as his wife. And Joseph went forth over the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.NLT: Then Pharaoh gave Joseph a new Egyptian name, Zaphenath-paneah. He also gave him a wife, whose name was Asenath. She was the daughter of Potiphera, the priest of On. So Joseph took charge of the entire land of Egypt.
1_41_45.WEB: Pharaoh called Joseph’s name Zaphenath-Paneah; and he gave him Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On as a wife. Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.CSB17: Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-paneah and gave him a wife, Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest at On. And Joseph went throughout the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.AMP: Then Pharaoh named Joseph Zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, priest of On (Heliopolis in Egypt), as his wife. And Joseph went out over all the land of Egypt [to inspect and govern it].
1_41_45.MSG: Then Pharaoh gave Joseph an Egyptian name, Zaphenath-Paneah (God Speaks and He Lives). He also gave him an Egyptian wife, Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, the priest of On (Heliopolis).And Joseph took up his duties over the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.NIV: Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-Paneah and gave him Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, to be his wife. And Joseph went throughout the land of Egypt.
1_41_45.NRSVCE: Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, as his wife. Thus Joseph gained authority over the land of Egypt.

1_41_46.NKJV: Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of Egypt.
1_41_46.KJV: And Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of Egypt.
1_41_46.ESV: Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt.
1_41_46.NASB: Now Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt.
1_41_46.NLT: He was thirty years old when he began serving in the court of Pharaoh, the king of Egypt. And when Joseph left Pharaoh’s presence, he inspected the entire land of Egypt.
1_41_46.WEB: Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt. Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went throughout all the land of Egypt.
1_41_46.CSB17: Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. Joseph left Pharaoh’s presence and traveled throughout the land of Egypt.
1_41_46.AMP: Now Joseph [had been in Egypt thirteen years and] was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. Joseph departed from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt [performing his duties].
1_41_46.MSG: Joseph was thirty years old when he went to work for Pharaoh the king of Egypt. As soon as Joseph left Pharaoh's presence, he began his work in Egypt.
1_41_46.NIV: Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from Pharaoh’s presence and traveled throughout Egypt.
1_41_46.NRSVCE: Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh, and went through all the land of Egypt.

1_41_47.NKJV: Now in the seven plentiful years the ground brought forth abundantly.
1_41_47.KJV: And in the seven plenteous years the earth brought forth by handfuls.
1_41_47.ESV: During the seven plentiful years the earth produced abundantly,
1_41_47.NASB: During the seven years of plenty the land brought forth abundantly.
1_41_47.NLT: As predicted, for seven years the land produced bumper crops.
1_41_47.WEB: In the seven plenteous years the earth produced abundantly.
1_41_47.CSB17: During the seven years of abundance the land produced outstanding harvests.
1_41_47.AMP: In the seven abundant years the earth produced handfuls [for each seed planted].
1_41_47.MSG: During the next seven years of plenty the land produced bumper crops.
1_41_47.NIV: During the seven years of abundance the land produced plentifully.
1_41_47.NRSVCE: During the seven plenteous years the earth produced abundantly.

1_41_48.NKJV: So he gathered up all the food of the seven years which were in the land of Egypt, and laid up the food in the cities; he laid up in every city the food of the fields which surrounded them.
1_41_48.KJV: And he gathered up all the food of the seven years, which were in the land of Egypt, and laid up the food in the cities: the food of the field, which was round about every city, laid he up in the same.

1_41_48.NASB: So he gathered all the food of [these] seven years which occurred in the land of Egypt and placed the food in the cities; he placed in every city the food from its own surrounding fields.
1_41_48.NLT: During those years, Joseph gathered all the crops grown in Egypt and stored the grain from the surrounding fields in the cities.
1_41_48.WEB: He gathered up all the food of the seven years which were in the land of Egypt, and laid up the food in the cities: the food of the field, which was around every city, he laid up in the same.
1_41_48.CSB17: Joseph gathered all the excess food in the land of Egypt during the seven years and put it in the cities. He put the food in every city from the fields around it.
1_41_48.AMP: And Joseph gathered all the [surplus] food of the seven [good] years in the land of Egypt and stored [enormous quantities of] the food in the cities. He stored away in every city the food [collected] from its own surrounding fields.
1_41_48.MSG: Joseph gathered up the food of the seven good years in Egypt and stored the food in cities. In each city he stockpiled surplus from the surrounding fields.
1_41_48.NIV: Joseph collected all the food produced in those seven years of abundance in Egypt and stored it in the cities. In each city he put the food grown in the fields surrounding it.
1_41_48.NRSVCE: He gathered up all the food of the seven years when there was plenty in the land of Egypt, and stored up food in the cities; he stored up in every city the food from the fields around it.

1_41_49.NKJV: Joseph gathered very much grain, as the sand of the sea, until he stopped counting, for it was immeasurable.
1_41_49.KJV: And Joseph gathered corn as the sand of the sea, very much, until he left numbering; for it was without number.
1_41_49.ESV: And Joseph stored up grain in great abundance, like the sand of the sea, until he ceased to measure it, for it could not be measured.
1_41_49.NASB: Thus Joseph stored up grain in great abundance like the sand of the sea, until he stopped measuring [it], for it was beyond measure.
1_41_49.NLT: He piled up huge amounts of grain like sand on the seashore. Finally, he stopped keeping records because there was too much to measure.
1_41_49.WEB: Joseph laid up grain as the sand of the sea, very much, until he stopped counting, for it was without number.
1_41_49.CSB17: So Joseph stored up grain in such abundance — like the sand of the sea — that he stopped measuring it because it was beyond measure.
1_41_49.AMP: Thus Joseph gathered and stored up grain in great abundance like the sand of the sea, until he stopped counting it, for it could not be measured.
1_41_49.MSG: Joseph collected so much grain—it was like the sand of the ocean!—that he finally quit keeping track.
1_41_49.NIV: Joseph stored up huge quantities of grain, like the sand of the sea; it was so much that he stopped keeping records because it was beyond measure.
1_41_49.NRSVCE: So Joseph stored up grain in such abundance — like the sand of the sea — that he stopped measuring it; it was beyond measure.

1_41_50.NKJV: And to Joseph were born two sons before the years of famine came, whom Asenath, the daughter of Poti-Pherah priest of On, bore to him.
1_41_50.KJV: And unto Joseph were born two sons before the years of famine came, which Asenath the daughter of Potipherah priest of On bare unto him. priest: or, prince

1_41_50.NASB: Now before the year of famine came, two sons were born to Joseph, whom Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, bore to him.
1_41_50.NLT: During this time, before the first of the famine years, two sons were born to Joseph and his wife, Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, the priest of On.

1_41_50.NRSVCE: Before the years of famine came, Joseph had two sons, whom Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, bore to him.

1_41_51.NKJV: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh: “For God has made me forget all my toil and all my father’s house.”
1_41_51.KJV: And Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh: For God, said he, hath made me forget all my toil, and all my father's house. Manasseh: that is, Forgetting
1_41_51.ESV: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh. “For,” he said, “God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house.”
1_41_51.NASB: Joseph named the firstborn Manasseh, 'For,' [he said], 'God has made me forget all my trouble and all my father's household.'
1_41_51.NLT: Joseph named his older son Manasseh, for he said, “God has made me forget all my troubles and everyone in my father’s family.”
1_41_51.WEB: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh, “For”, he said, “God has made me forget all my toil, and all my father’s house.”
1_41_51.CSB17: Joseph named the firstborn Manasseh and said, “God has made me forget all my hardship and my whole family.”
1_41_51.AMP: Joseph named the firstborn Manasseh (causing to forget), for he said, "God has made me forget all my trouble and hardship and all [the sorrow of the loss of] my father's household."
1_41_51.MSG: Joseph named the firstborn Manasseh (Forget), saying, "God made me forget all my hardships and my parental home."
1_41_51.NIV: Joseph named his firstborn Manasseh and said, “It is because God has made me forget all my trouble and all my father’s household.”
1_41_51.NRSVCE: Joseph named the firstborn Manasseh, “For,” he said, “God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father’s house.”

1_41_52.NKJV: And the name of the second he called Ephraim: “For God has caused me to be fruitful in the land of my affliction.”
1_41_52.KJV: And the name of the second called he Ephraim: For God hath caused me to be fruitful in the land of my affliction. Ephraim: that is, Fruitful
1_41_52.ESV: The name of the second he called Ephraim, “For God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.”
1_41_52.NASB: He named the second Ephraim, 'For,' [he said], 'God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.'
1_41_52.NLT: Joseph named his second son Ephraim, for he said, “God has made me fruitful in this land of my grief.”
1_41_52.WEB: The name of the second, he called Ephraim : “For God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.”
1_41_52.CSB17: And the second son he named Ephraim and said, “God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.”
1_41_52.AMP: He named the second [son] Ephraim (fruitfulness), for "God has caused me to be fruitful and very successful in the land of my suffering."
1_41_52.MSG: He named his second son Ephraim (Double Prosperity), saying, "God has prospered me in the land of my sorrow."
1_41_52.NIV: The second son he named Ephraim and said, “It is because God has made me fruitful in the land of my suffering.”
1_41_52.NRSVCE: The second he named Ephraim, “For God has made me fruitful in the land of my misfortunes.”

1_41_53.NKJV: Then the seven years of plenty which were in the land of Egypt ended,

1_41_53.NASB: When the seven years of plenty which had been in the land of Egypt came to an end,

1_41_53.CSB17: Then the seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt came to an end,

1_41_53.NIV: The seven years of abundance in Egypt came to an end,
1_41_53.NRSVCE: The seven years of plenty that prevailed in the land of Egypt came to an end;

1_41_54.NKJV: and the seven years of famine began to come, as Joseph had said. The famine was in all lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
1_41_54.KJV: And the seven years of dearth began to come, according as Joseph had said: and the dearth was in all lands; but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
1_41_54.ESV: and the seven years of famine began to come, as Joseph had said. There was famine in all lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
1_41_54.NASB: and the seven years of famine began to come, just as Joseph had said, then there was famine in all the lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
1_41_54.NLT: Then the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had predicted. The famine also struck all the surrounding countries, but throughout Egypt there was plenty of food.

1_41_54.CSB17: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. There was famine in every land, but in the whole land of Egypt there was food.

1_41_54.NRSVCE: and the seven years of famine began to come, just as Joseph had said. There was famine in every country, but throughout the land of Egypt there was bread.

1_41_55.NKJV: So when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Then Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, “Go to Joseph; whatever he says to you, do.”
1_41_55.KJV: And when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread: and Pharaoh said unto all the Egyptians, Go unto Joseph; what he saith to you, do.
1_41_55.ESV: When all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, “Go to Joseph. What he says to you, do.”
1_41_55.NASB: So when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried out to Pharaoh for bread; and Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, 'Go to Joseph; whatever he says to you, you shall do.'
1_41_55.NLT: Eventually, however, the famine spread throughout the land of Egypt as well. And when the people cried out to Pharaoh for food, he told them, “Go to Joseph, and do whatever he tells you.”

1_41_55.MSG: When the famine spread throughout Egypt, the people called out in distress to Pharaoh, calling for bread. He told the Egyptians, "Go to Joseph. Do what he tells you."

1_41_55.NRSVCE: When all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, “Go to Joseph; what he says to you, do.”

1_41_56.KJV: And the famine was over all the face of the earth: And Joseph opened all the storehouses, and sold unto the Egyptians; and the famine waxed sore in the land of Egypt. all the storehouses: Heb. all wherein was

1_41_56.NLT: So with severe famine everywhere, Joseph opened up the storehouses and distributed grain to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe throughout the land of Egypt.

1_41_56.CSB17: Now the famine had spread across the whole region, so Joseph opened all the storehouses and sold grain to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe in the land of Egypt.

1_41_57.NKJV: So all countries came to Joseph in Egypt to buy grain, because the famine was severe in all lands.
1_41_57.KJV: And all countries came into Egypt to Joseph for to buy corn; because that the famine was so sore in all lands.

1_41_57.NASB: [The people of] all the earth came to Egypt to buy grain from Joseph, because the famine was severe in all the earth.
1_41_57.NLT: And people from all around came to Egypt to buy grain from Joseph because the famine was severe throughout the world.

1_41_57.CSB17: Every land came to Joseph in Egypt to buy grain, for the famine was severe in every land.
1_41_57.AMP: And [the people of] all countries came to Egypt to Joseph to buy grain, because the famine was severe over all the [known] earth.

1_41_57.NIV: And all the countries came to Egypt to buy grain from Joseph, because the famine was severe in all the world.
1_41_57.NRSVCE: Moreover, all the world came to Joseph in Egypt to buy grain, because the famine became severe throughout the world.

1_42_1.NKJV: When Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, Jacob said to his sons, “Why do you look at one another?”
1_42_1.KJV: Now when Jacob saw that there was corn in Egypt, Jacob said unto his sons, Why do ye look one upon another?
1_42_1.ESV: When Jacob learned that there was grain for sale in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why do you look at one another?”
1_42_1.NASB: Now Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, and Jacob said to his sons, 'Why are you staring at one another?'
1_42_1.NLT: When Jacob heard that grain was available in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why are you standing around looking at one another?
1_42_1.WEB: Now Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, and Jacob said to his sons, “Why do you look at one another?”

1_42_1.NIV: Joseph’s Brothers Go to EgyptWhen Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why do you just keep looking at each other?”
1_42_1.NRSVCE: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why do you keep looking at one another?

1_42_2.NKJV: And he said, “Indeed I have heard that there is grain in Egypt; go down to that place and buy for us there, that we may live and not die.”

1_42_2.NLT: I have heard there is grain in Egypt. Go down there, and buy enough grain to keep us alive. Otherwise we’ll die.”

1_42_21.NKJV: Then they said to one another, “We are truly guilty concerning our brother, for we saw the anguish of his soul when he pleaded with us, and we would not hear; therefore this distress has come upon us.”
1_42_21.KJV: And they said one to another, We are verily guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the anguish of his soul, when he besought us, and we would not hear; therefore is this distress come upon us.
1_42_21.ESV: Then they said to one another, “In truth we are guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he begged us and we did not listen. That is why this distress has come upon us.”
1_42_21.NASB: Then they said to one another, 'Truly we are guilty concerning our brother, because we saw the distress of his soul when he pleaded with us, yet we would not listen; therefore this distress has come upon us.'
1_42_21.NLT: Speaking among themselves, they said, “Clearly we are being punished because of what we did to Joseph long ago. We saw his anguish when he pleaded for his life, but we wouldn’t listen. That’s why we’re in this trouble.”
1_42_21.WEB: They said to one another, “We are certainly guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he begged us, and we wouldn’t listen. Therefore this distress has come upon us.”

1_42_21.AMP: And they said to one another, "Truly we are guilty regarding our brother [Joseph], because we saw the distress and anguish of his soul when he pleaded with us [to let him go], yet we would not listen [to his cry]; so this distress and anguish has come on us."
1_42_21.MSG: Then they started talking among themselves. "Now we're paying for what we did to our brother—we saw how terrified he was when he was begging us for mercy. We wouldn't listen to him and now we're the ones in trouble."
1_42_21.NIV: They said to one another, “Surely we are being punished because of our brother. We saw how distressed he was when he pleaded with us for his life, but we would not listen; that’s why this distress has come upon us.”
1_42_21.NRSVCE: They said to one another, “Alas, we are paying the penalty for what we did to our brother; we saw his anguish when he pleaded with us, but we would not listen. That is why this anguish has come upon us.”

1_42_22.NKJV: And Reuben answered them, saying, “Did I not speak to you, saying, ‘Do not sin against the boy’; and you would not listen? Therefore behold, his blood is now required of us.”

1_42_22.ESV: And Reuben answered them, “Did I not tell you not to sin against the boy? But you did not listen. So now there comes a reckoning for his blood.”
1_42_22.NASB: Reuben answered them, saying, 'Did I not tell you, 'Do not sin against the boy'; and you would not listen? Now comes the reckoning for his blood.'
1_42_22.NLT: “Didn’t I tell you not to sin against the boy?” Reuben asked. “But you wouldn’t listen. And now we have to answer for his blood!”
1_42_22.WEB: Reuben answered them, saying, “Didn’t I tell you, saying, ‘Don’t sin against the child,’ and you wouldn’t listen? Therefore also, behold, his blood is required.”
1_42_22.CSB17: But Reuben replied: “Didn’t I tell you not to harm the boy? But you wouldn’t listen. Now we must account for his blood!”
1_42_22.AMP: Reuben answered them, "Did I not tell you, 'Do not sin against the boy'; and you would not listen? Now the accounting for his blood is required [of us for we are guilty of his death]."

1_42_36.NKJV: And Jacob their father said to them, “You have bereaved me: Joseph is no more, Simeon is no more, and you want to take Benjamin. All these things are against me.”

1_44_16.NKJV: Then Judah said, “What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how shall we clear ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants; here we are, my lord’s slaves, both we and he also with whom the cup was found.”
1_44_16.KJV: And Judah said, What shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants: behold, we are my lord's servants, both we, and he also with whom the cup is found.
1_44_16.ESV: And Judah said, “What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we clear ourselves? God has found out the guilt of your servants; behold, we are my lord's servants, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found.”
1_44_16.NASB: So Judah said, 'What can we say to my lord? What can we speak? And how can we justify ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants; behold, we are my lord's slaves, both we and the one in whose possession the cup has been found.'
1_44_16.NLT: Judah answered, “Oh, my lord, what can we say to you? How can we explain this? How can we prove our innocence? God is punishing us for our sins. My lord, we have all returned to be your slaves — all of us, not just our brother who had your cup in his sack.”
1_44_16.WEB: Judah said, “What will we tell my lord? What will we speak? Or how will we clear ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants. Behold, we are my lord’s slaves, both we, and he also in whose hand the cup is found.”

1_44_16.AMP: So Judah said, "What can we say to my lord? What can we reply? Or how can we clear ourselves, since God has exposed the sin and guilt of your servants? Behold, we are my lord's slaves, the rest of us as well as he with whom the cup is found."
1_44_16.MSG: Judah as spokesman for the brothers said, "What can we say, master? What is there to say? How can we prove our innocence? God is behind this, exposing how bad we are. We stand guilty before you and ready to be your slaves—we're all in this together, the rest of us as guilty as the one with the chalice."
1_44_16.NIV: “What can we say to my lord?” Judah replied. “What can we say? How can we prove our innocence? God has uncovered your servants’ guilt. We are now my lord’s slaves—we ourselves and the one who was found to have the cup.”

1_44_31.WEB: it will happen, when he sees that the boy is no more, that he will die. Your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant, our father, with sorrow to Sheol.
1_44_31.CSB17: when he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die. Then your servants will have brought the gray hairs of your servant our father down to Sheol in sorrow.
1_44_31.AMP: when he sees that the young man is not with us, he will die; and your servants will bring the gray hair of your servant our father down to Sheol in [great] sorrow.
1_44_31.MSG: the moment he realizes the boy is gone, he'll die on the spot. He'll die of grief and we, your servants who are standing here before you, will have killed him.
1_44_31.NIV: sees that the boy isn’t there, he will die. Your servants will bring the gray head of our father down to the grave in sorrow.
1_44_31.NRSVCE: when he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die; and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.

1_44_32.NKJV: For your servant became surety for the lad to my father, saying, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father forever.’
1_44_32.KJV: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.
1_44_32.ESV: For your servant became a pledge of safety for the boy to my father, saying, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father all my life.’
1_44_32.NASB: 'For your servant became surety for the lad to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him [back] to you, then let me bear the blame before my father forever.'
1_44_32.NLT: My lord, I guaranteed to my father that I would take care of the boy. I told him, ‘If I don’t bring him back to you, I will bear the blame forever.’
1_44_32.WEB: For your servant became collateral for the boy to my father, saying, ‘If I don’t bring him to you, then I will bear the blame to my father forever.’
1_44_32.CSB17: Your servant became accountable to my father for the boy, saying, ‘If I do not return him to you, I will always bear the guilt for sinning against you, my father.’
1_44_32.AMP: For your servant became security for the young man to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, then let me bear the blame before my father forever.'
1_44_32.MSG: And that's not all. I got my father to release the boy to show him to you by promising, 'If I don't bring him back, I'll stand condemned before you, Father, all my life.'
1_44_32.NIV: Your servant guaranteed the boy’s safety to my father. I said, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, I will bear the blame before you, my father, all my life!’
1_44_32.NRSVCE: For your servant became surety for the boy to my father, saying, ‘If I do not bring him back to you, then I will bear the blame in the sight of my father all my life.’

1_44_33.NKJV: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the lad as a slave to my lord, and let the lad go up with his brothers.
1_44_33.KJV: Now therefore, I pray thee, let thy servant abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.
1_44_33.ESV: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers.
1_44_33.NASB: 'Now, therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the lad a slave to my lord, and let the lad go up with his brothers.
1_44_33.NLT: “So please, my lord, let me stay here as a slave instead of the boy, and let the boy return with his brothers.
1_44_33.WEB: Now therefore, please let your servant stay instead of the boy, my lord’s slave; and let the boy go up with his brothers.
1_44_33.CSB17: Now please let your servant remain here as my lord’s slave, in place of the boy. Let him go back with his brothers.
1_44_33.AMP: Now, therefore, please let your servant (Judah) remain here instead of the youth [to be] a slave to my lord, and let the young man go home with his brothers.
1_44_33.MSG: "So let me stay here as your slave, not this boy. Let the boy go back with his brothers.
1_44_33.NIV: “Now then, please let your servant remain here as my lord’s slave in place of the boy, and let the boy return with his brothers.
1_44_33.NRSVCE: Now therefore, please let your servant remain as a slave to my lord in place of the boy; and let the boy go back with his brothers.

1_44_34.NKJV: For how shall I go up to my father if the lad is not with me, lest perhaps I see the evil that would come upon my father?”
1_44_34.KJV: For how shall I go up to my father, and the lad be not with me? lest peradventure I see the evil that shall come on my father. come: Heb. find my father
1_44_34.ESV: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the evil that would find my father.”
1_44_34.NASB: 'For how shall I go up to my father if the lad is not with me-- for fear that I see the evil that would overtake my father?'
1_44_34.NLT: For how can I return to my father if the boy is not with me? I couldn’t bear to see the anguish this would cause my father!”
1_44_34.WEB: For how will I go up to my father, if the boy isn’t with me? — lest I see the evil that will come on my father.”
1_44_34.CSB17: For how can I go back to my father without the boy? I could not bear to see the grief that would overwhelm my father.”
1_44_34.AMP: How can I go up to my father if the young man is not with me—for fear that I would see the tragedy that would overtake my [elderly] father [if Benjamin does not return]?"
1_44_34.MSG: How can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? Oh, don't make me go back and watch my father die in grief!"
1_44_34.NIV: How can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? No! Do not let me see the misery that would come upon my father.”
1_44_34.NRSVCE: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the suffering that would come upon my father.”

1_45_1.NKJV: Then Joseph could not restrain himself before all those who stood by him, and he cried out, “Make everyone go out from me!” So no one stood with him while Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_1.KJV: Then Joseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood by him; and he cried, Cause every man to go out from me. And there stood no man with him, while Joseph made himself known unto his brethren.
1_45_1.ESV: Then Joseph could not control himself before all those who stood by him. He cried, “Make everyone go out from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_1.NASB: Then Joseph could not control himself before all those who stood by him, and he cried, 'Have everyone go out from me.' So there was no man with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_1.NLT: Joseph could stand it no longer. There were many people in the room, and he said to his attendants, “Out, all of you!” So he was alone with his brothers when he told them who he was.
1_45_1.WEB: Then Joseph couldn’t control himself before all those who stood before him, and he cried, “Cause everyone to go out from me!” No one else stood with him, while Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_1.CSB17: Joseph could no longer keep his composure in front of all his attendants, so he called out, “Send everyone away from me!” No one was with him when he revealed his identity to his brothers.
1_45_1.AMP: Then Joseph could not control himself [any longer] in front of all those who attended him, and he called out, "Have everyone leave me." So no man stood there when Joseph revealed himself to his brothers.
1_45_1.MSG: Joseph couldn't hold himself in any longer, keeping up a front before all his attendants. He cried out, "Leave! Clear out—everyone leave!" So there was no one with Joseph when he identified himself to his brothers.
1_45_1.NIV: Joseph Makes Himself KnownThen Joseph could no longer control himself before all his attendants, and he cried out, “Have everyone leave my presence!” So there was no one with Joseph when he made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_1.NRSVCE: Then Joseph could no longer control himself before all those who stood by him, and he cried out, “Send everyone away from me.” So no one stayed with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.

1_45_2.NKJV: And he wept aloud, and the Egyptians and the house of Pharaoh heard it.
1_45_2.KJV: And he wept aloud: and the Egyptians and the house of Pharaoh heard . wept: Heb. gave forth his voice in weeping
1_45_2.ESV: And he wept aloud, so that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.
1_45_2.NASB: He wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard [it], and the household of Pharaoh heard [of it].
1_45_2.NLT: Then he broke down and wept. He wept so loudly the Egyptians could hear him, and word of it quickly carried to Pharaoh’s palace.
1_45_2.WEB: He wept aloud. The Egyptians heard, and the house of Pharaoh heard.
1_45_2.CSB17: But he wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard it, and also Pharaoh’s household heard it.
1_45_2.AMP: Joseph wept aloud, and the Egyptians [who had just left him] heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard of it.
1_45_2.MSG: But his sobbing was so violent that the Egyptians couldn't help but hear him. The news was soon reported to Pharaoh's palace.
1_45_2.NIV: And he wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard him, and Pharaoh’s household heard about it.
1_45_2.NRSVCE: And he wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.

1_45_3.NKJV: Then Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph; does my father still live?” But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed in his presence.

1_45_3.NASB: Then Joseph said to his brothers, 'I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?' But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed at his presence.

1_45_3.CSB17: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph! Is my father still living?” But they could not answer him because they were terrified in his presence.
1_45_3.AMP: Then Joseph said to his brothers, "I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?" But his brothers were speechless, for they were stunned and dismayed by [the fact that they were in] Joseph's presence.
1_45_3.MSG: Joseph spoke to his brothers: "I am Joseph. Is my father really still alive?" But his brothers couldn't say a word. They were speechless—they couldn't believe what they were hearing and seeing.

1_45_3.NRSVCE: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph. Is my father still alive?” But his brothers could not answer him, so dismayed were they at his presence.

1_45_5.NKJV: But now, do not therefore be grieved or angry with yourselves because you sold me here; for God sent me before you to preserve life.

1_45_5.NASB: 'Now do not be grieved or angry with yourselves, because you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.

1_45_7.NKJV: And God sent me before you to preserve a posterity for you in the earth, and to save your lives by a great deliverance.
1_45_7.KJV: And God sent me before you to preserve you a posterity in the earth, and to save your lives by a great deliverance. to preserve: Heb. to put for you a remnant
1_45_7.ESV: And God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors.
1_45_7.NASB: 'God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant in the earth, and to keep you alive by a great deliverance.
1_45_7.NLT: God has sent me ahead of you to keep you and your families alive and to preserve many survivors.
1_45_7.WEB: God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant in the earth, and to save you alive by a great deliverance.
1_45_7.CSB17: God sent me ahead of you to establish you as a remnant within the land and to keep you alive by a great deliverance.

1_45_7.MSG: God sent me on ahead to pave the way and make sure there was a remnant in the land, to save your lives in an amazing act of deliverance.
1_45_7.NIV: But God sent me ahead of you to preserve for you a remnant on earth and to save your lives by a great deliverance.
1_45_7.NRSVCE: God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors.

1_45_9.NKJV: “Hurry and go up to my father, and say to him, ‘Thus says your son Joseph: “God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not tarry.

1_45_9.CSB17: “Return quickly to my father and say to him, ‘This is what your son Joseph says: “God has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me without delay.

1_45_10.NASB: 'You shall live in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children's children and your flocks and your herds and all that you have.

1_45_11.NKJV: There I will provide for you, lest you and your household, and all that you have, come to poverty; for there are still five years of famine.” ’
1_45_11.KJV: And there will I nourish thee; for yet there are five years of famine; lest thou, and thy household, and all that thou hast, come to poverty.
1_45_11.ESV: There I will provide for you, for there are yet five years of famine to come, so that you and your household, and all that you have, do not come to poverty.’

1_45_11.NLT: I will take care of you there, for there are still five years of famine ahead of us. Otherwise you, your household, and all your animals will starve.’”

1_45_12.NKJV: “And behold, your eyes and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see that it is my mouth that speaks to you.

1_45_13.WEB: You shall tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that you have seen. You shall hurry and bring my father down here.”

1_45_16.NKJV: Now the report of it was heard in Pharaoh’s house, saying, “Joseph’s brothers have come.” So it pleased Pharaoh and his servants well.

1_45_16.NASB: Now when the news was heard in Pharaoh's house that Joseph's brothers had come, it pleased Pharaoh and his servants.

1_45_17.NKJV: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Say to your brothers, ‘Do this: Load your animals and depart; go to the land of Canaan.

1_45_26.NKJV: And they told him, saying, “Joseph is still alive, and he is governor over all the land of Egypt.” And Jacob’s heart stood still, because he did not believe them.

1_45_26.ESV: And they told him, “Joseph is still alive, and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt.” And his heart became numb, for he did not believe them.

1_45_26.NRSVCE: And they told him, “Joseph is still alive! He is even ruler over all the land of Egypt.” He was stunned; he could not believe them.

1_45_27.NKJV: But when they told him all the words which Joseph had said to them, and when he saw the carts which Joseph had sent to carry him, the spirit of Jacob their father revived.

1_45_28.NKJV: Then Israel said, “It is enough. Joseph my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die.”
